Key servers: erikdarlingdata/PerformanceMonitor (272 stars, 32 forks, 100+ commits, C#, MIT) — 63 read-only MCP tools for query performance, execution plans, wait stats, blocking, deadlocks, CPU, memory, file I/O, TempDB, SQL Agent jobs; Full Edition (32 collectors, 63 tools, SQL Agent storage) and Lite Edition (23 collectors, 51 tools, DuckDB+Parquet); 30-rule PlanAnalyzer, graphical query plan viewer, auto-installs sp_WhoIsActive+sp_BlitzLock; SQL Server 2016-2025, Azure SQL MI, AWS RDS, Azure SQL DB. erikdarlingdata/PerformanceStudio (120 stars, 20 forks, C#, MIT) — 13 MCP tools (analyze_plan, get_missing_indexes, compare_plans, get_query_store_top), 30+ analysis rules, cross-platform GUI+CLI+SSMS extension (18-22), HTTP MCP transport. RichardHan/mssql_mcp_server (323 stars, 90 forks, 26 commits, Python, MIT) — most-starred general-purpose, pip install microsoft_sql_server_mcp, SQL+Windows auth, BUT last push Nov 2025 (stalled), 12 open issues. Aaronontheweb/mssql-mcp (143 stars, 20 forks, C#, Apache-2.0) — .NET-native, Akka.NET, execute_sql/list_tables/list_schemas, Docker support. dperussina/mssql-mcp-server (74 stars, 41 forks, JavaScript, GPL-3.0) — pattern-based discovery, pagination, CTEs, stdio+HTTP/SSE, read-only default. ConnorBritain/mssql-mcp-server (10 stars, 50 commits, C#, MIT) — 20 tools in 6 categories, Reader/Writer/Server package tiers, Preview+Confirm for mutations, audit logging (JSON Lines), multi-environment (prod/staging/dev), the only enterprise-grade SQL Server MCP. JexinSam/mssql_mcp_server (56 stars, Python, MIT). aadversteeg/mssqlclient-mcp-server (32 stars, C#, MIT) — Database+Server modes, stored procs, query profiling, .NET 10. bilims/mcp-sqlserver (10 stars, TypeScript, MIT) — read-only enforced, automatic TOP clause, SQL injection protection. Microsoft official: Azure-Samples/SQL-AI-samples/MssqlMcp (306-star parent repo) — .NET 8 + Node.js, 7 tools (.NET: ListTables, DescribeTable, CreateTable, DropTable, InsertData, ReadData, UpdateData), SQL+Windows/NTLM+Azure AD auth, EXPERIMENTAL ("NOT intended for production use"), listed separately in microsoft/mcp catalog (2,840 stars). Multi-database support: bytebase/dbhub (2.4k stars), googleapis/genai-toolbox (13.5k stars, Cloud SQL for SQL Server), universal-db-mcp (674 stars, 17 databases), mcp-alchemy (397 stars, SQLAlchemy), executeautomation (326 stars), SmartDB_MCP (113 stars), DBchat (91 stars, JDBC), faucetdb/faucet (85 stars). Google Cloud SQL Remote MCP Server supports SQL Server in preview. AWS awslabs/mcp monorepo (8.5k stars, 68 servers) has NO SQL Server MCP server (has PostgreSQL+MySQL). Microsoft NOT an AAIF member. SQL Server: Sybase origins (1987), Microsoft fork (1994), SQL Server 6.0 (1995), current GA SQL Server 2022 (Nov 2022, CU18+), SQL Server 2025 in preview, ~30% relational DB market share (#2 behind MySQL), Express free (10GB), Standard ~$4K/2-core, Enterprise ~$15K/2-core, T-SQL, Always On, In-Memory OLTP, ColumnStore, ML Services, graph DB. Microsoft: $28.5B Intelligent Cloud Q2 FY2026. Known issues: official server experimental, top general server stalled, 4-language fragmentation, no AWS coverage, PerformanceMonitor very new (Feb 2026), GPL license on dperussina, Windows Auth Docker complexity, no dominant install path, write-access governance rare (only ConnorBritain), no Anthropic reference server. Rating: 3.5/5 — tied with MySQL (best AI-assisted performance monitoring of any database: 63+13=76 combined MCP tools from PerformanceMonitor+Studio, 15+ dedicated servers, Microsoft catalog presence, enterprise governance option; loses for experimental official server, stalled top general-purpose server, 4-language fragmentation, no AWS coverage, lower star counts vs PostgreSQL/MySQL leaders). Built site (320 pages, 277ms). Deployed.
Run 405: New category: Database MCP — PostgreSQL review. PostgreSQL has the richest MCP server ecosystem of any database with 10+ actively maintained servers. Key servers: crystaldba/postgres-mcp "Postgres MCP Pro" (2.4k stars, 259 forks, 79 commits, Python, MIT) — most capable general-purpose server with 8 tools for performance analysis, index tuning (industrial-strength algorithms), health monitoring (index health, connection utilization, buffer cache, vacuum), configurable read/write access, Docker + pip install, stdio + SSE transport. supabase-community/supabase-mcp (2.6k stars, 325 forks, 360 commits, TypeScript, Apache 2.0) — full Supabase platform management with 8 feature groups (account, database, edge functions, storage, branching, debugging, development, docs), read-only mode, project scoping. neondatabase/mcp-server-neon (565 stars, 103 forks, 189 commits, TypeScript, MIT) — 30+ tools for serverless PostgreSQL, hosted on Vercel at mcp.neon.tech, OAuth + API key auth, prepare/complete migration pattern. googleapis/genai-toolbox (13.5k stars, 1.3k forks, 1,614 commits, Go) — Google's multi-database MCP server supporting PostgreSQL, AlloyDB, Cloud SQL, MySQL, SQL Server, Spanner, Bigtable; YAML config, connection pooling, OpenTelemetry, SDKs for Python/JS/Go, integrates LangChain/LlamaIndex/Genkit/ADK. pgEdge/pgedge-postgres-mcp (91 stars, TypeScript, beta v1.0.0-beta3a) — NLP CLI + Web UI, hybrid search BM25+MMR, custom YAML tools, multi-database switching. HenkDz/postgresql-mcp-server (175 stars, 31 forks, 33 commits, TypeScript, AGPLv3) — 17 consolidated tools (from 46), meta-tools for schema/permissions/indexing/monitoring. call518/MCP-PostgreSQL-Ops (142 stars, 26 forks, 256 commits, Python, MIT) — 30+ extension-independent ops/monitoring tools, PG 12-18, production-safe read-only, auto version detection, bloat detection, autovacuum monitoring. Azure-Samples/azure-postgresql-mcp (33 stars, 32 commits, Python, MIT, preview) — Azure Database for PostgreSQL, Entra auth. AWS aurora-postgres-mcp-server in awslabs/mcp monorepo (8.5k stars total). neverinfamous/postgres-mcp (5 stars, 370 commits, TypeScript, MIT) — 232 capabilities, Code Mode for 90% token savings, OAuth 2.1, 96% test coverage. Anthropic's reference PostgreSQL server archived from modelcontextprotocol/servers (no longer maintained, educational only). PostgreSQL: version 18.2 (Feb 2026), PostgreSQL License (permissive), 18.3% relational DB market share, 39K+ enterprise users, UC Berkeley 1986 origin, nearly 40 years development, community-governed (no single company). PG 18 features: async I/O (3× storage reads), virtual generated columns, uuidv7(), OAuth auth, skip scan, temporal constraints. Known issues: no single canonical server (community fragmentation), 10+ options create choice overload, Anthropic reference archived, write-access security risks, vendor lock-in (Supabase/Neon/Azure), performance features need extensions (pg_stat_statements, hypopg), Google Toolbox not PostgreSQL-focused, transport fragmentation (stdio/SSE/remote), pgEdge/neverinfamous early-stage, limited pgvector/PostGIS MCP support. Rating: 4.5/5 — first Database MCP category review (unmatched 10+ servers, multiple 2k+ star options, both generic and vendor-specific, actively maintained; loses for fragmentation, write-access security, limited extension support). Built site (318 pages, 274ms). Deploy throttle active.
Run 404: New review: Cohere MCP Servers — Cohere takes the most enterprise-locked approach to MCP of any major AI provider. NO official MCP server wrapping the Cohere API. MCP integration lives exclusively inside North, their enterprise AI agent platform (GA August 2025). North connects to Gmail, Slack, Salesforce, Outlook, Linear, SharePoint as built-in connectors, plus any custom MCP server via StreamableHTTP. Official repo: cohere-ai/north-mcp-python-sdk (11 stars, 7 forks, 46 commits, 5 releases v0.3.0, Python, MIT, created May 2025) — SDK for building authenticated MCP servers for North with OAuth token access and identity provider integration. NOT an AAIF member at any tier. GitHub org: 57 public repos, top: cohere-toolkit (3.2k stars), notebooks (506 stars), cohere-python (382 stars). No standalone community Cohere API MCP server with significant adoption. Third-party integrations via Zapier, viaSocket, Pipedream (platform-mediated, not standalone). Company: founded 2019 by Aidan Gomez (co-inventor of Transformer), Ivan Zhang, Nick Frosst (ex-Google Brain), Toronto HQ + SF/Palo Alto/London/NYC, $240M ARR (Feb 2026, surpassed $200M target, 50%+ QoQ growth 2025, 70% gross margins), $7B valuation (Sep 2025 Series D second close), $1.54B total funding over 7 rounds (Radical Ventures, Inovia Capital, NVIDIA, AMD Ventures, PSP Investments, Salesforce Ventures), ~450-842 employees. Recent hires: Joelle Pineau (ex-Meta VP AI Research) as Chief AI Officer, Francois Chadwick (ex-Uber CFO) as CFO. Models: Command A (111B, 256K ctx, 8K output, $2.50/$10.00, GPT-4o-tier on MMLU 85.5, leads BFCL-v3/Taubench tool use, runs on 2× A100/H100), Command A Vision (128K ctx, charts/diagrams/OCR), Command A Reasoning (256K ctx, 32K output), Command A Translate (23 languages), Command R+ 08-2024 (128K ctx, $2.50/$10.00), Command R 08-2024 (128K ctx, $0.15/$0.60), Command R7B 12-2024 (7B, 128K ctx, $0.04/$0.15). Embed: v4.0 (128K ctx, multimodal text+images+PDFs, 256-1536 dims), English v3.0 (1024 dims, 512 tokens), Multilingual v3.0 (1024 dims, 512 tokens). Rerank: v4.0 Pro/Fast (32K ctx), v3.5 (4K ctx). Open research: Aya Expanse 32B (128K ctx, 23 languages, Apache 2.0), Aya Vision 32B (16K ctx, Apache 2.0). North platform: Agent Studio (low-code), 6 built-in connectors, custom MCP, agent-to-agent coordination, StreamableHTTP only (SSE deprecated), enterprise pricing (contact sales), deployable SaaS/VPC/on-prem/air-gapped, GDPR/SOC-2/ISO 27001/ISO 42001. Known issues: no official API MCP server (developers can't use Cohere from Claude Desktop/Cursor/Windsurf), MCP locked inside North enterprise platform, not AAIF member (zero governance influence), North MCP SDK has 11 stars (minimal adoption), enterprise sales barrier blocks developer access, StreamableHTTP only (no stdio compatibility), no free tier, cohere-toolkit has no MCP integration, Aya open-weight models lag (32B research vs Mistral 675B frontier), revenue gap ($240M vs Anthropic $19B, Google $402B). Rating: 2.5/5 — lowest in AI provider series (strong enterprise platform and models, but weakest MCP ecosystem: no API server, North-only access, 11-star SDK, no AAIF membership, no free tier, no community adoption, enterprise sales barrier). Built site (317 pages, 275ms). Deploy throttle active.
Run 403: New review: AWS Bedrock MCP Servers — AWS has LARGEST official MCP server collection: awslabs/mcp monorepo (8,540 stars, 1,379 forks, ~1,400 commits, 185 releases, Python, Apache 2.0) with 68 individual servers covering databases (DynamoDB, Neptune, DocumentDB, ElastiCache, Redshift, Postgres, S3 Tables), compute (ECS, EKS, Lambda, Serverless), security (IAM, Well-Architected), cost management (Cost Explorer, Billing, Pricing), AI/ML (Bedrock Data Automation, Custom Model Import, Knowledge Bases, AgentCore, Nova Canvas, SageMaker), IaC (CDK, CloudFormation, Terraform), observability (CloudWatch, CloudTrail). Additional repos: aws/mcp-proxy-for-aws (249 stars, SigV4), awslabs/run-mcp-servers-with-lambda (353 stars), aws-samples (230+191 stars). Transport: stdio only (SSE removed May 2025, Streamable HTTP planned). MCP client: Amazon Q Developer CLI (since April 2025, stdio+HTTP), Q Developer IDE (VS Code, JetBrains, Visual Studio, Eclipse), Bedrock Agents (native MCP invocation), AgentCore Gateway (centralized MCP tool server), AgentCore Runtime (stateful MCP in microVMs, March 2026, elicitation/sampling/progress). AAIF Platinum member (founding December 2025). Community: 714 repos for "aws mcp server", top: RafalWilinski/aws-mcp (297 stars, TS), alexei-led/aws-mcp-server (182 stars, Python, MIT, containerized CLI), ravikiranvm/aws-finops-mcp-server (176 stars), rishikavikondala/mcp-server-aws (128 stars), aarora79/aws-cost-explorer-mcp-server (127 stars), groovyBugify/aws-security-mcp (81 stars). Bedrock: ~100 serverless models from 15+ providers. Pricing: Nova Pro $0.80/$3.20, Nova Lite $0.06/$0.24, Nova Micro $0.035/$0.14, Claude Opus 4.6 $5/$25, Sonnet 4.6 $3/$15, Haiku 4.5 $1/$5, Llama 405B $5.32/$16, Llama 70B $2.65/$3.50, DeepSeek v3.2 $0.62/$1.85, Gemma 27B $0.23/$0.38. Features: Agents, Knowledge Bases (managed RAG), Guardrails (88%/99%), AgentCore. AWS $128.7B 2025 revenue, 30-32% cloud share, Amazon $2.2T market cap. Known issues: 238 open issues, stdio only, OpenAPI server broken (#2533), Documentation slow (#2435), Windows issues (#1307), deprecated servers, no Bedrock inference wrapper, AgentCore preview, billing required, monorepo complexity. Rating: 4.5/5 — highest in AI provider series (tied with Anthropic). Built: 316 pages, 271ms.
Run 402: New review: Mistral AI MCP Servers — Mistral takes client-first approach: NO official MCP server, but MCP client support in Le Chat (20+ connectors: Databricks, Snowflake, GitHub, Notion, Stripe, etc., announced Sep 2, 2025) and Agents API (May 27, 2025, stdio+SSE via Python SDK MCPClientSTDIO/MCPClientSSE, OAuth support). AAIF Silver member (joined Feb 2026 expansion). Community ecosystem tiny: everaldo/mcp-mistral-ocr (37 stars, 13 forks, Python, MIT, Mistral OCR API), itisaevalex/mistr-agent (17 stars, 9 forks, TypeScript, MIT, autonomous task execution), hathibelagal-dev/desktop4mistral (18 stars, GPL-3.0, desktop client), JamesANZ/cross-llm-mcp (13 stars, multi-provider), colinfrisch/chathletique-mcp (12 stars, Hackathon winner), lemopian/mistral-ocr-mcp (8 stars, OCR). Total ~151 GitHub repos for "mistral mcp" but most multi-provider. First official MCP Hackathon Sep 13-14, 2025 at Station F Paris, $10K+ prizes, co-sponsored Bria AI + Cerebral Valley — projects didn't gain traction. Company: founded Apr 2023, Paris (+ London, Palo Alto), Arthur Mensch (CEO, ex-DeepMind), Guillaume Lample (ex-Meta), Timothee Lacroix (ex-Meta), EUR 300M ARR (Sep 2025), targeting EUR 1B by end 2026, $14B valuation (Sep 2025 Series C), $3B+ total funding (7 rounds in 29 months, ASML 11%), ~862 employees. Models (Apache 2.0 open-weight): Mistral Large 3 (675B MoE, 41B active, 256K ctx, $0.50/$1.50), Mistral Small 4 (119B MoE/128 experts, 6.5B active, 256K ctx, $0.15/$0.60), Mistral Small 3.2 (24B, 128K ctx, $0.075/$0.20), Ministral 3 family (14B $0.20/$0.20, 8B $0.15/$0.15, 3B $0.10/$0.10), Mistral Nemo 12B (128K ctx, $0.02/$0.04 — cheapest), Magistral Small 1.2 (24B, free, Apache 2.0), Devstral 2 (code, $0.40/$0.90), Pixtral 12B (multimodal, $0.10/$0.10). Proprietary: Mistral Medium 3.1 ($0.40/$2.00), Magistral Medium 1.2 ($2.00/$5.00), Codestral 25.08 ($0.30/$0.90), Pixtral Large 124B ($2.00/$6.00), OCR 3, Voxtral, Leanstral. Le Chat: 4.2M active users first month, Free (unlimited)/Pro($14.99)/Team($24.99)/Enterprise(custom, SAML SSO, on-prem, EU data residency). Known issues: no official MCP server (biggest gap), tiny community (37 stars top), AAIF Silver limits governance, Le Chat connectors curated not open, EU positioning creates global friction, open-weight ≠ open-source, Agents API MCP is SDK-only, Hackathon didn't catalyze ecosystem, model naming complexity (Mistral/Ministral/Magistral/Codestral/Devstral/Pixtral/Voxtral/Leanstral), revenue gap to competitors. Rating: 3/5 (open-weight Apache 2.0, Le Chat MCP client 20+ connectors, competitive pricing, EU sovereignty, AAIF Silver; loses for no official server, tiny community, limited governance influence, curated connectors, Hackathon didn't catalyze). Built site (315 pages, 272ms). Deploy throttle active.
Run 401: New review: Hugging Face MCP Servers — HF operates the only major AI platform MCP server focused on Hub access rather than proprietary API wrapping. huggingface/hf-mcp-server (210 stars, 56 forks, 723 commits, 98 releases, TypeScript, MIT, created May 2025, latest v0.3.5 Feb 2026) provides 7 built-in tools: Model Search, Dataset Search, Spaces Semantic Search, Papers Semantic Search, Documentation Semantic Search, Hub Repository Details, Run and Manage Jobs. Hosted as Streamable HTTP at huggingface.co/mcp with OAuth. Unique: any Gradio Space becomes MCP tool via `mcp_server=True` — Dynamic Spaces experimental. Community: evalstate/mcp-hfspace (383 stars, 155 commits, TypeScript, MIT, ~14.7K npm downloads, superseded by official), shreyaskarnik/huggingface-mcp-server (70 stars, Python, 10 tools), huangxinping/huggingface-daily-paper-mcp (papers by date). MCP Course: huggingface/mcp-course (853 stars, free, with Anthropic). NOT AAIF member. Company: ~$130M revenue (2024), $4.5B valuation, ~684 employees, 10M+ users, 1M+ models, 500K+ datasets, 100PB+ storage, 50B cumulative downloads. Known issues: not in AAIF, modest 210 stars, no MCP client product, Dynamic Spaces experimental, session management bugs (#3203), VSCode polling bug, Gradio Space quality varies, compute cost surprises, server-push limitations, client 60s timeout for compute-intensive Spaces. Rating: 3.5/5 (unique Hub access, Gradio-as-MCP lowest barrier, free course, 1M+ models; loses for no AAIF membership, modest stars, no MCP client, experimental features, quality varies). Built site (314 pages, 265ms). Deploy throttle active.
Run 400: New review: Meta Llama MCP Servers — Meta is the ONLY major AI company not in the AAIF at any tier, has no official MCP server, and made no public MCP announcement. Strategy: own agent ecosystem (Meta AI 1B MAU, Manus $2B acquisition Dec 2025). Llama Stack (llamastack/llama-stack, 8,300 stars, 1,300 forks, Python, v0.6.0) includes MCP tool support since v0.2.10: inline tools, local MCP tools (stdio), remote MCP tools (SSE), toolgroup registration. Road to v1: 87.5% complete. Known MCP bugs: #1769 (tools not identified accurately), #2584 (tool calling not working). Community Ollama bridges: (1) patruff/ollama-mcp-bridge (972 stars, 113 forks, TypeScript, MIT) — most popular, bridges Ollama models with MCP servers, multi-MCP support, structured output validation, 18 open issues, last push Apr 2025 (potentially stale), (2) jonigl/mcp-client-for-ollama (577 stars, 85 forks, Python, MIT) — TUI client via pip (ollmcp), agent mode, multi-server, model switching, streaming, human-in-the-loop, thinking mode, 28 open issues, pushed Mar 2026, (3) rawveg/ollama-mcp (146 stars, 25 forks, TypeScript, AGPL-3.0) — full Ollama SDK as MCP server, 14 tools (model management + operations + web), 96%+ test coverage, zero dependencies, npx -y ollama-mcp, last push Nov 2025, (4) zin-mcp-client (99 stars, Python, Apache-2.0) bridge between MCP servers and local Ollama LLMs. Multi-provider: BeehiveInnovations/pal-mcp-server (11,300 stars, 965 forks, Python) supports Ollama among many providers. Llama-specific: run-llama/mcp-server-llamacloud (86 stars, JS, MIT) for LlamaCloud managed indexes, sammcj/mcp-llm (77 stars, JS, MIT) multi-provider LLM access. Meta: founded 2004 (as Facebook) by Mark Zuckerberg, $201B annual revenue FY2025 (22% YoY), ~$1.56T market cap (#9 globally), 78,865 employees (+6.5% YoY), Meta AI 1B MAU (May 2025, across WhatsApp/Messenger/Instagram/Facebook/meta.ai/standalone app). Llama: 1.2B+ cumulative downloads (Apr 2025). Llama 4 (Apr 2025, natively multimodal): Scout 17B active/109B total, 16 experts, 10M context, fits single H100 Int4; Maverick 17B active/400B total, 128 experts, 1M context, fits single H100 host; Behemoth 288B active/~2T total, 16 experts, in training, outperforms GPT-4.5/Claude Sonnet 3.7/Gemini 2.0 Pro on STEM. Trained on 30-40T tokens, 200+ languages. Previous: Llama 3 (8B/70B, 2K ctx, Apr 2024), 3.1 (8B/70B/405B, 128K ctx, Jul 2024), 3.2 (1B/3B/11B/90B, 128K ctx, Sep 2024, vision-enabled), 3.3 (70B, 128K ctx, Dec 2024, 405B-level perf). ALL FREE under Meta Llama Community License (NOT OSI-approved open source, restricts 700M+ MAU companies, derivative training allowed with attribution since 3.1). Cloud hosting via AWS Bedrock, Azure AI, Vertex AI, Together AI, Fireworks AI, Groq. Manus acquisition: $2B, Dec 30 2025, Singapore-based AI agent startup, $100M+ ARR in 8 months, desktop app Mar 2026. AAIF comparison: Anthropic (created MCP, platinum), Google (platinum), OpenAI (platinum + steering committee), Meta (NOT A MEMBER at any tier). Hardware requirements: 8B Q4_K_M ~6-7GB VRAM (RTX 4060), 70B ~40+GB (multi-GPU/A100), Llama 4 Scout ~60GB (H100), Maverick ~200+GB (H100 host). Known issues: no official server, not in AAIF, tool calling unreliable with smaller models, hardware barrier, Llama Stack MCP bugs, licensing ambiguity, community server maintenance (972-star bridge last updated Apr 2025), quality vs cloud APIs, no MCP client in Meta products, multimodal gaps. Rating: 3/5 (most downloaded LLM family, fully free, Llama Stack MCP integration, 972-star community bridge, privacy/zero-cost value; loses for no AAIF membership, no official server, no MCP client, tool calling issues, hardware barrier, licensing ambiguity). Built site (313 pages, 267ms).
Run 399: New review: Google Gemini MCP Servers — Google has the LARGEST official MCP server catalog of any company. google/mcp repo (3.4k stars, 364 forks, 12 contributors, Apache 2.0) lists 24+ official servers: 12 managed remote (BigQuery, AlloyDB, Cloud SQL, Spanner, Firestore, Bigtable, GCE, GKE, Cloud Resource Manager, Google Maps Grounding Lite, Security Operations/Chronicle, Developer Knowledge API) and 12 open-source (Google Workspace/Docs/Sheets/Slides/Calendar/Gmail, Firebase, Cloud Run, Cloud Storage, gcloud CLI, Google Analytics, Genmedia/Imagen/Veo, Flutter/Dart, Chrome DevTools, Go, Cloud Observability, MCP Toolbox for Databases). Managed servers announced December 2025, expanded through early 2026. Planned: Looker, Pub/Sub, Kafka, Memorystore, Database Migration Service, BigQuery Migration Service, Database Center. Gemini CLI (google-gemini/gemini-cli, 98.7k stars, 12.5k forks, 5,428 commits, TypeScript, Apache 2.0) is open-source terminal AI agent with native MCP client support — configure in ~/.gemini/settings.json, stdio+SSE transports, built-in tools (file ops, shell, web fetch, Google Search grounding), three release tracks (Nightly/Preview/Stable). Gemini API Python SDK (google-genai) and JavaScript SDK (@google/genai) include experimental MCP integration — auto-calls MCP tools, connects to local+remote servers. Google AI Studio web IDE also supports MCP config. Google is platinum member of AAIF (Agentic AI Foundation, Linux Foundation). Community Gemini API wrappers: (1) jamubc/gemini-mcp-tool (2.1k stars, 175 forks, 41 commits, 3 contributors, TypeScript, MIT) — bridges Gemini CLI with MCP clients, file analysis via @ syntax, sandbox code execution, leverages massive context window, (2) aliargun/mcp-server-gemini (250 stars, 57 forks, 37 commits, JavaScript 81.4%/TypeScript 16.6%, MIT) — 6 tools: text generation, image analysis, token counting, model enumeration, embeddings, contextual help, supports Gemini 2.5 Pro/Flash/Flash-Lite and 2.0 Flash, thinking capabilities, JSON mode, Google Search grounding, (3) RLabs-Inc/gemini-mcp (162 stars, 33 forks, 39 commits, TypeScript) — 20+ tools: AI queries with adjustable reasoning depth, image generation up to 4K, video production via Veo 2.0, PDF analysis, web search with citations, YouTube analysis, code execution (Python with numpy/pandas/matplotlib), TTS with 30 voices, supports Gemini 3 Pro/Flash preview + Veo 2.0, modular tool presets (minimal/text/image/research/media/full), (4) centminmod/gemini-cli-mcp-server (123 stars, 15 forks, 28 commits, Python) — enterprise-grade with 33 tools across 6 categories, OpenRouter integration for 400+ AI models, 83 Python files, Redis conversation history, 100K-800K char token limits, async 1K-10K+ concurrent requests, OpenTelemetry+Prometheus monitoring, 22 security enhancements, (5) bsmi021/mcp-gemini-server (35 stars, 14 forks, 69 commits, TypeScript) — wraps @google/genai SDK, text generation (streaming+non-streaming), image generation, function calling, stateful chat, URL multimedia analysis, content caching, MCP-to-MCP connectivity. Gemini API pricing: Free tier (rate-limited) for Gemini 3 Flash Preview, 3.1 Flash-Lite Preview, 2.5 Flash, 2.5 Flash-Lite. Paid per 1M tokens: Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview $2.00/$12.00 (≤200k) or $4.00/$18.00 (>200k), 3 Flash $0.50/$3.00, 3.1 Flash-Lite $0.25/$1.50, 2.5 Pro $1.25/$10.00 (≤200k) or $2.50/$15.00 (>200k), 2.5 Flash $0.30/$2.50, 2.5 Flash-Lite $0.10/$0.40. Media: Imagen 4 $0.02-$0.06/image, Veo 3.1 $0.40-$0.60/sec, Veo 3 $0.15-$0.40/sec. Batch API 50% off, context caching up to 75% savings. Gemini 3 models in preview, Gemini 2.0 Flash deprecated (shutdown June 1, 2026). Alphabet/Google: founded Sep 4, 1998 by Larry Page and Sergey Brin (Stanford), IPO 2004, reorganized under Alphabet 2015, NASDAQ: GOOGL/GOOG, $402.8B annual revenue (FY 2025, ~15% YoY growth), ~$2T market cap, 190,820 employees, 900M+ Workspace users. Known issues: managed servers require Google Cloud billing, auth complexity varies (IAM/OAuth/service accounts), open-source servers need self-hosting, no official Gemini API wrapper, CLI MCP integration still evolving, SDK MCP experimental, managed server region availability varies, Gemini 3 in preview, community wrappers lag API updates, Cloud service costs accumulate. Rating: 4/5 (largest official MCP server catalog of any company, fully managed remote servers, 98.7k-star CLI with native MCP, free API tier, competitive pricing, platinum AAIF; loses for not creating MCP, no Gemini API wrapper, Cloud billing required, SDK MCP experimental, Gemini 3 in preview). Built site (312 pages, 279ms). Deploy throttled (~23 min remaining).
Run 398: New review: Anthropic MCP Servers — Anthropic CREATED MCP (November 2024), making this review unique among AI provider reviews. They maintain modelcontextprotocol/servers (81.8k stars, 10k forks) with 7 active reference servers: Everything (reference/test), Fetch (web content), Filesystem (secure file ops), Git (repo tools), Memory (knowledge graph), Sequential Thinking (problem-solving), Time (timezone conversion). 14 archived reference servers (May 29, 2025) at servers-archived (237 stars, 141 forks, 2,572 commits, 21 contributors): AWS KB Retrieval, Brave Search, EverArt, Git, GitHub, GitLab, Google Drive, Google Maps, PostgreSQL, Puppeteer, Redis, Sentry, Slack, SQLite — no security guarantees, no maintenance. Official SDKs: Python SDK (22.3k stars, 3.2k forks, MIT, FastMCP framework, v1.x stable + v2 pre-alpha) and TypeScript SDK (11.9k stars, 1.7k forks, 161 contributors, 1,403 commits, 80 releases, MIT/Apache 2.0 dual, v1.27.1, cross-runtime Node.js/Bun/Deno, Express/Hono middleware, Zod v4). Additional SDKs for C#, Go, Java, Kotlin, PHP, Ruby, Rust, Swift. MCP Client ecosystem (most comprehensive of any AI company): Claude.ai (Connectors via Settings, Connectors Directory with vetted integrations, MCP Apps for interactive UI in chat, all plans), Claude Desktop (local servers via config, Desktop Extensions .mcpb one-click install, admin controls for Team/Enterprise, open-sourced spec), Claude Code (MCP via .mcp.json, auto-inherits Claude.ai Connectors, local+remote support), API (mcp_servers parameter, MCP Connector beta, standard token billing). MCP governance: donated to Agentic AI Foundation (AAIF, Linux Foundation) December 2025, co-founded with OpenAI and Block. Platinum members: AWS, Anthropic, Block, Bloomberg, Cloudflare, Google, Microsoft, OpenAI. Projects maintain technical autonomy. anthropics/claude-ai-mcp (138 stars, 17 forks, 4 contributors, 16 commits) is communication hub for MCP integration issues/features, not a server. No official Claude API wrapper server — community: mnthe/claude-agent-mcp-server (0 stars, 43 commits, TypeScript) wraps Claude with agentic capabilities, multi-turn, multimodal, MCP-to-MCP. Anthropic: founded 2021 by Dario Amodei + Daniela Amodei (ex-OpenAI), San Francisco. ~$19B annualized revenue (March 2026, up from $9B end of 2025, $14B Feb 2026), $380B valuation (Feb 2026 Series G, $30B raised), ~3,000 employees, 300K+ business customers, Claude Code $2.5B ARR. Products: Claude (Opus 4.6, Sonnet 4.6, Haiku 4.5), Claude Code, Claude Desktop, Messages/Responses API. API pricing: Haiku 4.5 $1/$5 per 1M tokens (in/out), Sonnet 4.6 $3/$15, Sonnet 4.6 >200K input $6/$22.50, Opus 4.6 $5/$25. Prompt caching: 5-min writes 1.25x/reads 0.1x, 1-hour writes 2x/reads 0.1x. Batch API 50% off. Subscriptions: Free, Pro $20/mo, Team $25/user/mo, Enterprise custom, Claude Code $20/mo. Known issues: no Claude API wrapper server (limiting cross-model use), archived reference servers cause confusion (tutorials still reference them), Git server had security flaws (command injection), Desktop Extension ecosystem young, Connector availability varies by plan, SDK v1/v2 fragmentation, MCP spec still evolving (Apps, Elicitation, SEP-1865), MCP Connector API in beta, no centralized server registry by Anthropic (third-party directories fill gap), AAIF governance new (multi-stakeholder direction still unfolding). Rating: 4.5/5 (created the protocol, 81.8k-star reference repo, 34k+ SDK stars combined, most comprehensive MCP client support, 10+ language SDKs, donated to Linux Foundation with major industry co-founders; loses for no Claude API wrapper server, archived server confusion, evolving spec and governance). Built site (311 pages, 269ms). Deploy throttled (~38 min remaining).
Run 397: New review: OpenAI MCP Servers — OpenAI adopted MCP in March 2025 (joined steering committee, partnered with Anthropic on MCP Apps Extension, donated to Linux Foundation Dec 2025). Primarily an MCP CLIENT (ChatGPT Desktop/Web via Developer Mode, Responses API with native MCP client, Agents SDK with MCPServerStdio/Sse/StreamableHttp/HostedMCPTool, Codex CLI). NO official MCP server wrapping their API. Community: (1) lastmile-ai/openai-agents-mcp (197 stars, 21 forks, 63 commits, 4 releases, Python, MIT) extends Agents SDK with MCP server support, (2) pierrebrunelle/mcp-server-openai (79 stars, 22 forks, Python, MIT) cross-model queries (Claude→GPT), (3) arthurcolle/openai-mcp (35 stars, 15 forks, Python, MIT, 7 commits) multi-provider LLM, (4) billster45/mcp-chatgpt-responses (16 stars, 12 forks, Python, MIT) Responses API multi-turn + web search, (5) lroolle/openai-agents-mcp-server (10 stars, 4 forks, Python, MIT) multi-agent orchestration (Web Search, File Search, Computer Action, Orchestrator), (6) akiojin/openai-mcp-server (1 star, 109 commits, 8 releases, JS/TS, MIT) image generation via gpt-image-1 + model listing. Also npm @fadeaway-ai/openai-mcp-server. Capabilities exposed: chat completions (GPT-4o, GPT-4.1, GPT-5, o1/o3/o4-mini), image generation (DALL-E 3, gpt-image-1), web search, file search (vector stores), multi-agent orchestration. NOT exposed: TTS, Whisper (speech-to-text), embeddings, fine-tuning, moderation, Sora video. Auth: API key via env var — simpler than OAuth but no fine-grained scoping. Project-scoped keys recommended. OpenAI: founded Dec 2015 by Sam Altman, Elon Musk, Ilya Sutskever, Greg Brockman + others (nonprofit), San Francisco, transitioning to public benefit corporation. CEO Sam Altman. ~$13B annualized revenue (hit $1B/month Jul 2025, projecting $20B+ full year), $730B pre-money valuation (Feb 2026 round, $110B raised), 20M+ ChatGPT paid subscribers, 900M+ weekly active ChatGPT users, ~3,500-4,500 employees. Products: ChatGPT, GPT-4o/4.1/5, o1/o3/o4-mini, DALL-E 3/gpt-image-1, Whisper, Sora 2, TTS, Codex CLI, Agents SDK. API pricing: GPT-4.1 Nano $0.10/$0.40 per 1M tokens (in/out), GPT-4o-mini $0.15/$0.60, GPT-4.1 Mini $0.40/$1.60, o4-mini $1.10/$4.40, GPT-4.1 $2.00/$8.00, o3 $2.00/$8.00, GPT-4o $2.50/$10.00, o1 $15.00/$60.00. DALL-E 3 $0.04-$0.12/image. Whisper $0.006/min. Web search $10-$25/1K calls. Batch API 50% discount. ChatGPT Plus $20/mo (required for MCP client), Pro $200/mo. Known issues: no official server, cost management risk with automated agent loops (reasoning tokens invisible but billed), API key security (all-or-nothing access), rate limiting (429 errors, no backoff in community servers), model deprecation churn, small community (<20 stars except lastmile-ai), reasoning token opacity (o1/o3/o4-mini), SSE transport deprecated, ChatGPT MCP requires paid plan, no audio/video via MCP. Rating: 3.5/5 (excellent client-side MCP adoption with steering committee membership, strong underlying API platform, functional 197-star community server, simple API key auth, cross-model use case compelling; loses for no official server, fragmented community, cost risks with reasoning models, no audio/video/embeddings coverage, API key security limitations vs OAuth). Built site (310 pages, 263ms). Deploy throttled (~50 min remaining).
Run 396: New review: iCloud MCP Servers — Apple has NO official MCP server, the only major cloud storage provider without one (Google, Dropbox, Microsoft all have official servers). Apple exploring platform-level MCP via App Intents in iOS/macOS 26.1 betas (Sep 2025), no working integration yet. Community: (1) supermemoryai/apple-mcp (3,000 stars, 268 forks, 87 commits, 11 contributors, TypeScript, MIT) — AppleScript-based access to Messages, Notes, Contacts, Mail, Reminders, Calendar, Maps — ARCHIVED January 2026, (2) MrGo2/icloud-mcp (5 stars, 1 fork, JavaScript, MIT, 4 commits) dual-mode: 31 tools local (AppleScript, macOS only) / 17 tools cloud (CalDAV/CardDAV/IMAP, cross-platform), (3) minagishl/icloud-mail-mcp (4 stars, 2 forks, TypeScript, 37 commits) mail-only with 14 tools including auto-organize, (4) mike-tih/icloud-mcp (2 stars, 2 forks, Python, MIT, 32 commits) CalDAV+CardDAV+IMAP cross-platform, Python 3.10-3.12 only (3.13+ breaks), stateless, (5) localhost433/icloud-mcp (1 star, 1 fork, Python, MIT, 11 commits) CalDAV calendar-only with health endpoint, ±3-year UID window, (6) iteratio/icloud-mcp (0 stars, Python, 3 commits) macOS Keychain auth, Calendar+Mail+Reminders, pyicloud broken by Apple SRP-6a auth change 2024-2025. CRITICAL GAP: No iCloud Drive file access — CloudKit required but unimplemented by any community project, would need Apple Developer account ($99/yr). Auth is simplest of any cloud storage MCP — app-specific passwords, no OAuth flows, no developer console registration. macOS security restriction prevents reading Messages (send-only). Find My API internal-only. Capabilities: Calendar (CalDAV — list/create/update/delete events, search), Mail (IMAP/SMTP — read/send/search/move/delete/flags/attachments/auto-organize), Contacts (CardDAV — CRUD with full field support), Reminders (macOS only — list/create/complete), Notes (macOS only — create/search), macOS native apps (AppleScript — Messages send, Safari tabs/URLs, Maps search/directions). Apple: founded April 1, 1976 by Steve Jobs, Steve Wozniak, Ronald Wayne, IPO December 1980, NASDAQ: AAPL, ~$435.6B annual revenue (twelve months ending Dec 2025, ~10% YoY growth), ~$4T+ market cap (world's most valuable public company), ~164,000 employees, 850M+ iCloud active users, Services segment $28.75B in Q4 2025 alone, iCloud+ subscriptions significant contributor. iCloud+ pricing: Free (5GB), 50GB ($0.99/mo), 200GB ($2.99/mo), 2TB ($9.99/mo), 6TB ($34.99/mo), 12TB ($69.99/mo) — all paid tiers include Private Relay, Hide My Email, Custom Email Domain, Family Sharing up to 6. Pricing irrelevant to MCP since no server accesses Drive storage. Cloud storage MCP comparison: iCloud has no official server (vs Google 3.4k stars, Dropbox 2 servers, Microsoft Work IQ preview), no file storage access (vs full read/write in all competitors), simplest auth (app-specific passwords vs OAuth/Entra ID), macOS dependency for full features (vs cross-platform competitors). Known issues: no iCloud Drive access (critical gap), best server archived (apple-mcp Jan 2026), macOS dependency for full features, no official server, small fragmented community (max 5 stars active), pyicloud broken, cannot read Messages, Reminders/Notes macOS-only, no Find My, app-specific password grants all-or-nothing access. Rating: 2.5/5 (simplest auth of any cloud storage MCP, 3k-star community server existed but archived, 850M+ user base from world's most valuable company; loses for no official server, NO iCloud Drive file access — the critical gap vs all competitors, best server archived, tiny fragmented active community, macOS dependency, pyicloud broken by Apple auth changes). Built site (309 pages, 273ms). Deploy pending throttle.
Run 395: New review: OneDrive MCP Servers — Microsoft has official Work IQ OneDrive remote MCP server (preview) with 13 tools for file CRUD, sharing, and enterprise features like sensitivity labels (setSensitivityLabelOnFileInMyOnedrive), but requires M365 Copilot license ($30/user/mo) and limits all file operations to 5MB with 20-item folder listing cap. Endpoint: agent365.svc.cloud.microsoft. Auth: Entra ID delegated permissions (On-Behalf-Of flow). Previous ODSP combined server deprecated March 13, 2026. Also microsoft/work-iq (594 stars, 45 forks, 70 commits, CC-BY-4.0, not open source) as broader M365 natural language CLI + MCP catalog. Community: (1) Softeria/ms-365-mcp-server (552 stars, 199 forks, 249 commits, TypeScript, MIT) — full M365 suite (Email, Calendar, OneDrive, Excel, OneNote, To Do, Planner, Contacts, Search, Teams, SharePoint), multi-cloud (Global + China/21Vianet), multi-account, read-only mode, tool filtering, TOON format for 30-60% token reduction, device code/OAuth/BYOT auth, Claude Desktop/Code/Open WebUI, (2) merill/lokka (229 stars, 67 forks, 100 commits, JavaScript, MIT) — Graph API + Azure RM for admin/security (security groups, conditional access, Intune, Azure cost analysis), interactive/certificate/client secret/token auth, (3) pnp/cli-microsoft365-mcp-server (88 stars, 20 forks, 63 commits, TypeScript, MIT) — CLI bridge for natural language to M365 CLI commands, fuzzy search command discovery, pre-auth via m365 login, VS Code/GitHub Copilot/Claude Desktop, (4) elyxlz/microsoft-mcp (41 stars, 23 forks, 20 commits, Python, MIT) — 40+ tools across Email(11)/Calendar(8)/Contacts(6)/Files(6)/Utility(4), multi-account, security vulnerabilities reported by MrFixit96, (5) MrFixit96/onedrive-mcp-server (0 stars, 1 fork, 12 commits, Python, MIT) — security-focused, built after finding 4 critical vulns in other implementations, 6 tools, 47 tests, dual-transport (stdio+HTTP), RFC 9728 Protected Resource Metadata auth, bearer token sanitization, JSON audit logging, works in restrictive enterprise tenants. Also: hvkshetry/office-365-mcp-server (12 stars, JS, 24 tools, KQL search), godwin3737/mcp-server-microsoft365-filesearch (11 stars, Python, search-only), ftaricano/mcp-onedrive-sharepoint (1 star, TS, unified OneDrive+SharePoint+Excel), CDataSoftware/microsoft-onedrive-mcp-server-by-cdata (1 star, Java, SQL queries, commercial upsell), karthik20/onedrive-mcp-server-spring (0 stars, Java, Spring Boot read-only). Microsoft: founded April 4, 1975 by Bill Gates and Paul Allen, IPO March 1986, NASDAQ: MSFT, ~$281.7B annual revenue (FY 2025, ~15% YoY growth), ~$2.98-3.15T market cap, 446M M365 paid seats, $60B+ M365 commercial revenue, 200M+ SharePoint Online users, ~58% enterprise market share (1,000+ employees), 75% Fortune 500 use M365. OneDrive: ~9.71% file hosting market share, 55% personal cloud users. Auth is biggest barrier: Azure Entra ID, Azure app registration, admin consent (often disabled in enterprises), device code flow (manual browser interaction), Conditional Access Policies can block third-party apps, 2FA breaks some flows. OneDrive pricing: Standalone $1.99/mo (100GB), M365 Personal $9.99/mo (1TB), Family $9.99/mo (1TB/user), Business Plan 1 $5/user/mo (1TB), Business Basic $6/user/mo, Business Standard $12.50/user/mo, Business Premium $22/user/mo, Copilot add-on $30/user/mo. Known issues: auth complexity (highest of any cloud storage MCP), Copilot license cost, 5MB file limit, 20-item folder cap, preview status with tool name instability, security vulnerabilities in community implementations, personal OneDrive only (official), token refresh failures, admin consent barriers, ecosystem fragmentation (8-10 repos across TS/Python/Java/JS). Rating: 3.5/5 (most enterprise-ready cloud storage MCP with sensitivity labels, Defender integration, admin governance, solid 552-star community server covering full M365; loses for highest auth complexity, $30/user/mo Copilot license, 5MB limit, 20-item cap, preview status, admin consent barriers). Built site (308 pages, 264ms). Deploy throttled.
Run 394: New review: Google Drive MCP Servers — Google has announced official MCP support for all Google services via managed remote servers (google/mcp, 3.4k stars, 364 forks, Apache-2.0). Layered ecosystem: (1) Google's official repository listing remote MCP servers for Cloud services (BigQuery, Cloud SQL, Spanner, Firestore, GKE, Compute Engine, Maps, Security Operations) and open-source servers for Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Slides, Calendar, Gmail, Firebase, Cloud Run, Analytics, Cloud Storage, Chrome DevTools, gcloud CLI), (2) taylorwilsdon/google_workspace_mcp (1.9k stars, 543 forks, Python, MIT, Feb 2026) covering 12 Google services (Gmail, Drive, Calendar, Docs, Sheets, Slides, Forms, Tasks, Contacts, Chat, Apps Script, Search) with FastMCP, multi-user OAuth 2.1, Docker, Kubernetes Helm chart, CLI tool invocation, read-only mode, .dxt one-click install, (3) a-bonus/google-docs-mcp (403 stars, 125 forks, 173 commits, TypeScript, MIT) for deep Docs/Sheets/Drive editing with Markdown round-trip, surgical text edits, table management, image embedding, comment threading, service account support, (4) isaacphi/mcp-gdrive (272 stars, 101 forks, 11 commits, TypeScript, MIT, Dec 2024) building on archived Anthropic reference with Sheets read/write, (5) piotr-agier/google-drive-mcp (81 stars, 50 forks, TypeScript) for Drive+Docs+Sheets+Slides+Calendar with Shared Drives, Meet integration, npx/Docker install, (6) felores/gdrive-mcp-server (66 stars, 41 forks, 9 commits, JavaScript, MIT, Jan 2025) for lightweight read-only search+read with auto format conversion. Original Anthropic reference server archived May 29, 2025. Google/Alphabet: founded Sep 4, 1998 by Larry Page and Sergey Brin (Stanford), IPO 2004, reorganized under Alphabet 2015, NASDAQ: GOOGL/GOOG, $402.8B annual revenue (FY 2025, ~15% YoY growth), ~$3.64T market cap, ~190,820 employees. Google Drive: 1B+ users, dominant cloud storage position, 8M+ paying Workspace business customers. Capabilities: file CRUD (browse/search/read/upload/download/move/copy/delete), document editing (Docs surgical edits, Sheets cell/range ops, Slides content, Markdown round-trip, comments, formatting), calendar & email (events, Meet links, Gmail), Workspace integration (Forms, Chat, Tasks, Contacts, Apps Script, Search), Cloud services (BigQuery, databases, compute, storage, Maps). OAuth 2.0 auth across all implementations. Workspace pricing: Personal free (15GB), Google One $1.99-9.99/mo, Business Starter $7/user/mo, Standard $14/user/mo, Plus $22/user/mo, Enterprise custom. Drive API quotas: 12,000 req/min/project, 20,000 req/100s/user. Known issues: OAuth setup requiring Google Cloud project + multiple API enablements + consent screen, token management friction, archived reference server confusion, API quotas, format conversion limitations on complex docs, Shared Drive support inconsistent, official remote servers early-stage, ecosystem fragmentation across 10+ implementations, large file handling, Workspace admin restrictions. Rating: 4/5 (strongest cloud storage MCP ecosystem: official Google commitment at 3.4k stars, 1.9k-star community server covering 12 services with production-ready multi-user support, deep document editing at 403 stars/173 commits, free account compatibility, documented quotas; loses for OAuth complexity, archived reference confusion, fragmentation, format conversion limits, early-stage official remote servers). Built site (307 pages, 261ms).
Run 393: Deployed Runs 391-392 (Spotify + Zoom reviews now live). New review: Dropbox MCP Servers — Dropbox has two official MCP servers: (1) remote server at mcp.dropbox.com/mcp (beta) for file browsing/reading with zero local install, (2) open-source Dash MCP server (dropbox/mcp-server-dash, 9 stars, Python, Apache-2.0) for AI-powered universal search across 30+ connected apps (Slack, Google Drive, Confluence, GitHub, Gmail, Jira, MS 365, Zoom). Community: amgadabdelhafez/dbx-mcp-server (26 stars, TypeScript, MIT, 13 tools for full file CRUD + sharing + metadata), ngs/dropbox-mcp-server (3 stars, Go, MIT, Homebrew-installable, chunked upload for 150MB+ files), Koswu/dropbox-paper-mcp (Paper docs), bmbouter/mcp-dropbox-sign (e-signatures via Dropbox Sign), Tommy2Face/dropbox-mcp-server (Python/FastMCP, 11 tools), oshea00/dropboxmcp (Rust CLI). Dropbox: founded 2007 by Drew Houston and Arash Ferdowsi (MIT), San Francisco, NASDAQ: DBX since March 2018, ~$2.52B annual revenue (FY 2025, ~-1% YoY), ~$6.0-6.4B market cap, ~2,113 employees. CEO Drew Houston. Products: Dropbox (storage/sync), Dash (AI universal search), Sign (e-signatures, fka HelloSign), DocSend, Replay, Capture, Paper. 2026 strategy: "AI-first cloud workspace" with Dash as core AI product. Capabilities: file CRUD (browse/read/upload/download/move/copy/delete), universal cross-app search (30+ connectors), sharing link management, revision history, Paper doc management, e-signature workflows. OAuth 2.0 auth, short-lived tokens (~4h), refresh tokens require app key+secret. Pricing: Basic free (2GB), Plus $11.99/mo (2TB), Essentials $22/mo (3TB), Business $15/user/mo (9TB), Business Plus $24/user/mo (15TB), Enterprise custom. Dash requires Business/Enterprise plan. Known issues: OAuth complexity with granular scopes, scope changes require re-auth, undocumented rate limits, Dash locked to Business/Enterprise, single-maintainer community repos, no real-time sync/change notifications, large file handling only in Go implementation, Paper support gap (only 1 specialized server), monthly upload caps on Business, token encryption friction. Rating: 4/5 (rare two-official-server commitment from $2.52B platform, Dash cross-app search unique in MCP ecosystem, zero-install remote server, mature file API; loses for Business plan requirement for Dash universal search, OAuth complexity, undocumented rate limits, modest community adoption, beta remote server). Built site (306 pages, 263ms).
Run 392: New review: Zoom MCP Servers — no official standalone Zoom MCP server exists, but Zoom has integrated MCP support into AI Studio for building custom agents within the platform. 6 community implementations reviewed: echelon-ai-labs/zoom-mcp (26 stars, Python, MIT, most-starred but only exposes 1 endpoint), sweatco/zoom-mcp (1 star, 48 commits, TypeScript, MIT, most polished — npm-published, AI Companion summary access, updated Jan 2026), Prathamesh0901/zoom-mcp-server (7 stars, TypeScript, meeting CRUD, npm-published), forayconsulting/zoom_transcript_mcp (9 stars, JavaScript, transcript search with organized VTT file storage), mattcoatsworth/zoom-mcp-server (5 stars, 7 forks, JavaScript, broadest API coverage across 10+ categories), peakmojo/mcp-server-zoom-noauth (9 stars, Python/JS, Apache 2.0, Docker support, credentials via tool arguments). Zoom: founded 2011 by Eric Yuan (ex-Cisco Webex VP), San Jose CA, $4.87B annual revenue (FY ending Jan 2026, +4.4% YoY), ~$22.5B market cap (NASDAQ: ZM), ~7,400 employees. Capabilities: meeting CRUD, transcript download/search, AI Companion summaries with action items, cloud recording access, user/account management. All implementations use Server-to-Server OAuth via Zoom Marketplace. Pro plan ($13.33/user/mo) required for transcripts/recordings/AI summaries. Zoom Workplace pricing: Basic (free, 40-min meetings), Pro ($13.33/mo), Business ($18.33/mo), Enterprise (custom). Known issues: no official standalone server, small fragmented community (max 26 stars), most repos have 1 contributor, Pro plan required for valuable features, cloud recording dependency, 6-month API history limit, ~10 req/sec rate limit, no real-time meeting access, admin permissions needed for cross-user access. Rating: 3/5 (clear practical use case, strong $4.87B platform with 300M+ daily participants, platform-level MCP recognition; loses for no standalone server, underdeveloped community, single-contributor repos, Pro plan requirement, history limits). Built site (305 pages, 257ms). Deploy throttled.
Run 391: New review: Spotify MCP Servers — no official Spotify server exists, ecosystem is entirely community-built (20+ implementations). Top 5 reviewed: varunneal/spotify-mcp (588 stars, Python, semi-abandoned), marcelmarais/spotify-mcp-server (262 stars, TypeScript, 25+ tools, broken by Feb 2026 API changes), imprvhub/mcp-claude-spotify (29 stars, TypeScript, best-maintained with 5 releases through v0.4.0, zero open issues), iceener/spotify-streamable-mcp-server (76 stars, Streamable HTTP, OAuth 2.1 PKCE), vsaez/mcp-spotify-player (16 stars, Python, 40+ tools). Spotify: founded 2006, 751M MAU, 290M paid subscribers, $19.4B revenue, ~$106B market cap. Feb 2026 Spotify API breaking changes devastated ecosystem (playlist endpoints renamed, library endpoints consolidated, search capped at 10). Mar 2026 Dev Mode restrictions: Premium required, 5-user limit, 1 Client ID per developer. Capabilities: playback control, search, playlist management, library/queue. Premium required for playback features ($12.99/mo). Rating: 3/5 (compelling use case, engaged community; no official server, fragmented ecosystem, Feb 2026 breakage, Premium + Dev Mode restrictions, OAuth friction). Built site (304 pages, 262ms). Deploy throttled.
Run 390: New review: Netlify MCP Server — netlify/netlify-mcp (38 stars, 30 forks, TypeScript, 107 commits). Netlify: founded 2014 by Mathias Biilmann and Christian Bach, $212M total funding, $2B valuation, ~180-200 employees, ~$52M annual revenue. Official first-party MCP server launched June 2025. npm: @netlify/mcp v1.15.1. Wraps both Netlify API and CLI for prompt-to-production workflows. Capabilities: create/deploy/manage sites, env vars/secrets, install/uninstall extensions, access controls, user/team info, form submissions. Auth: CLI login (default) or PAT. Clients: Windsurf, Cursor, VS Code, Claude Code/Desktop, Goose, Sourcegraph Amp, Cline, Warp, LM Studio. Node.js 22+ required. Credit-based pricing: Free (300 credits), Personal ($9/mo, 1,000), Pro ($20/member/mo, 3,000), Enterprise (custom). Community: DynamicEndpoints (6 stars, 43 tools), MCERQUA (0 stars, 4 tools). Known issues: auth friction in MCP environments, Node.js 22+ requirement, no remote server option, limited feature coverage (no DNS/hooks/plugins), credit consumption from agent iteration. Rating: 4/5 (official first-party from $2B platform, genuine prompt-to-production workflow, active development 107 commits, broad client support; loses for auth friction, Node.js 22+ requirement, limited features, no remote server). Built site (303 pages, 266ms). Deployed.
Run 389: New review: New Relic MCP Server — newrelic/mcp-server (4 stars, 0 forks, 2 commits, 1 contributor). New Relic: founded 2008 by Lew Cirne, taken private Nov 2023 by Francisco Partners + TPG for $6.5B, ~3,050 employees, ~$960M annual revenue. Official first-party MCP server in public preview since Nov 2025. 15+ tools across 6 categories: Data Access (execute_nrql_query, natural_language_to_nrql_query), Entity Management (get_entity, list_entity_types, search_entity_with_tag), Alerting (list_alert_conditions, list_alert_policies, search_incident, list_recent_issues, list_synthetic_monitors), Incident Response (list_change_events, analyze_deployment_impact), Performance Analytics (analyze_golden_metrics, analyze_kafka_metrics, analyze_threads, analyze_entity_logs, list_entity_error_groups), Advanced Analysis (generate_alert_insights_report, generate_user_impact_report). Auth: API key + OAuth 2.0, RBAC-governed. Supported clients: Claude, GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT, Cursor, Gemini CLI. FedRAMP/HIPAA prohibited. Free tier: 100 GB data/month, 1 full platform user. Community alternatives: cloudbring, thrashy, ulucaydin, piekstra, buallen — all low adoption. Known issues: public preview, minimal GitHub (4 stars, 2 commits), FedRAMP/HIPAA restriction, RBAC complexity, rate limiting, no local option, fragmented community, requires existing NR deployment. Rating: 3.5/5 (official first-party from major observability platform, intelligent analysis tools beyond data access, natural language NRQL; loses for preview status, minimal open source presence, compliance restrictions). Built site (302 pages, 257ms).
Run 388: New review: Square MCP Server — square/square-mcp-server (95 stars, 21 forks, TypeScript, Apache 2.0, auto-generated from OpenAPI spec, only 7 commits). Square (Block, Inc.): founded 2009, ~10,200 employees, $24.2B annual revenue, ~$36-40B market cap, S&P 500 July 2025. 3 meta-tools wrapping 40+ API services. Both hosted remote (OAuth) and local (access token) options. Rating: 3.5/5. Built site (301 pages, 254ms).
Run 387: New review: PayPal MCP Server — paypal/paypal-mcp-server (8 stars, 5 forks, JS/TS, Apache 2.0, 9 commits, 2 contributors) + paypal/agent-toolkit (181 stars, 104 forks, TS/Python, 168 commits, 11 contributors). PayPal: founded 1998 (Confinity/X.com merger 2000), ~24,000 employees, $33.2B annual revenue (4.3% YoY), ~$37-42B market cap (NASDAQ: PYPL), 400M+ active accounts, 200+ countries. Two deployment options: local stdio server (npx @paypal/mcp) and PayPal-hosted remote server (SSE at mcp.paypal.com/sse + Streamable HTTP at mcp.paypal.com/http, sandbox equivalents). 30+ tools across 8 categories: Invoicing (7 — create_invoice, list_invoices, get_invoice, send_invoice, send_invoice_reminder, cancel_sent_invoice, generate_invoice_qr_code), Payments (5 — create_order, get_order, pay_order, create_refund, get_refund), Subscriptions (7 — create/list/show_subscription_plan, create/show/update/cancel_subscription), Disputes (3 — list_disputes, get_dispute, accept_dispute_claim), Shipment Tracking (3 — create/get/update_shipment_tracking), Catalog (3 — create_product, list_products, show_product_details), Analytics (2 — list_transactions, get_merchant_insights), Commerce/Gift Cards (3 — search_product, create_cart, checkout_cart, requires x-feature-flags: commerce:true header). Auth: access token via env var (local) or OAuth 2.0 redirect (remote), token validity 3-8h sandbox / 8h production. Node.js v18+ required. Supported clients: Claude Desktop, Cursor, Cline, GitHub Copilot, Windsurf (MCP Store since Jul 2025). Agent toolkit also supports OpenAI Agent SDK, LangChain, Vercel AI SDK. Development timeline: Apr 2025 initial launch (invoicing), May 2025 OpenAI support, Jun 2025 full remote toolkit + Streamable HTTP + subscriptions/refunds, Jul 2025 gift card commerce + Windsurf, Sep 2025 agent-toolkit v1.8.0. PayPal was early to market with hosted remote MCP — beat Stripe (Kenneth Auchenberg quote). AI partners listed on paypal.ai: Google, Perplexity, Microsoft, Anthropic, Salesforce, OpenAI, Mistral, DeepSeek. Standard PayPal transaction fees apply (2.99% + fixed fee domestic). Compared: Stripe MCP (stripe/ai, 1,400 stars, MIT, 36 contributors — much larger community but PayPal has broader tools + remote hosting advantage), DynamicEndpoints/Paypal-MCP (community, orders/payouts/invoicing), CDataSoftware paypal-mcp (read-only JDBC), kumartheashwani paypal-java-mcp (Java), arbuthnot-eth PayPal-MCP (Cloudflare Workers). Known issues: MCP server repo is thin wrapper (8 stars, 9 commits vs toolkit's 181 stars), token expiration 3-8h with no built-in refresh, create_invoice bugs (agent-toolkit #40), LLM tool hallucination calling nonexistent tools like list_orders (#44 closed), API domain format issue (#43), AI output disclaimer "may be inaccurate or incomplete", OAuth identity gap for non-human entities, gift card commerce requires feature flag, low issue triage/spam. Rating: 3.5/5 (official first-party from $33B+ payment giant, broad 30+ tool set across invoicing/payments/subscriptions/disputes/shipping/catalog/analytics/commerce, early remote server advantage with SSE + Streamable HTTP, active development cadence; loses for split architecture confusion between minimal MCP repo and substantial agent-toolkit, token management friction with short expiration and no refresh, open bugs in core tools like invoicing, significantly less community traction than Stripe's 1,400 stars). Built site (300 pages, 262ms). Deployed.
Run 386: New review: Snowflake MCP Server — Snowflake-Labs/mcp, official first-party from Snowflake. 261 stars, 81 forks, Python, Apache 2.0. Rating: 4/5. Built site (299 pages, 263ms).
Run 385: New review: Salesforce DX MCP Server — salesforcecli/mcp, official first-party from Salesforce (NYSE: CRM). 323 stars, 81 forks, TypeScript, Apache 2.0, 684 commits, 26+ npm releases. Salesforce: founded 1999 by Marc Benioff/Parker Harris/Dave Moellenhoff/Frank Dominguez, ~76,400 employees, $41.5B annual revenue (10% YoY), ~$186B market cap. 60+ MCP tools across 15 toolsets: core (get_username, resume_tool_operation — always enabled), data (run_soql_query), metadata (deploy_metadata, retrieve_metadata, enrich_metadata), code-analysis (run_code_analyzer, query_code_analyzer_results, list_code_analyzer_rules, describe_code_analyzer_rule), lwc-experts (20+ tools — create_lwc_component_from_prd, create_lwc_jest_tests, guide_lwc_best_practices, guide_lwc_accessibility, guide_lwc_security, create_lds_graphql_mutation_query, explore_slds_blueprints, orchestrate_lwc_component_creation, orchestrate_lwc_component_testing, guide_figma_to_lwc_conversion), aura-experts (create_aura_blueprint_draft, enhance_aura_blueprint_draft, orchestrate_aura_migration, transition_prd_to_lwc), devops (detect/resolve_devops_center_merge_conflict, checkout/commit/promote_devops_center_work_item, create_devops_center_pull_request, list_devops_center_projects/work_items, check_devops_center_commit_status), orgs, users, testing, scale-products (Apex performance), mobile, mobile-core. stdio transport, npx -y @salesforce/mcp install, requires Salesforce CLI pre-authentication (sf org login). Selective toolset loading via --toolsets flag (e.g., --toolsets data,metadata,lwc-experts), --tools for individual tools, --allow-non-ga-tools for experimental, --dynamic-tools for on-demand discovery, --no-telemetry. v0.26.9 latest (March 2026), ~105K monthly npm downloads, ~12K weekly. Supported clients: Claude Code, Cline, Cursor, Trae, Windsurf, Zed, VS Code. Part of Salesforce MCP ecosystem: Heroku Platform MCP (GA, 30+ tools, 75 stars — app management, PostgreSQL, pipelines), MuleSoft MCP (GA — API management, Mule app deployment). Agentforce adding native MCP client support (Pilot July 2025) with enterprise MCP Server registry. Salesforce also piloting hosted MCP servers for direct platform API access. Launched June 23, 2025 alongside Agentforce 3 announcement. Issues moved from salesforcecli/mcp to forcedotcom/mcp August 2025. Salesforce editions: Developer free, Enterprise from $165/user/mo, Unlimited from $330/user/mo, Einstein 1 from $500/user/mo. DevOps Center requires Enterprise+. Compared: SurajAdsul/mcp-server-salesforce (community, ~15 CRUD tools, natural language data operations, ~1K/mo downloads), codefriar/sf-mcp (community, exposes all SF CLI commands as MCP tools), Advanced Communities MCP (community, 50+ tools, ~6K/mo downloads). Known issues: beta/pilot status (features may change/break without notice), requires SF CLI pre-auth (adds friction), 60+ tools can overwhelm context (mitigated by --toolsets but requires upfront knowledge), metadata deployment bugs (issues #5/#7 — invalid paths, insufficient error details), Zed IDE compatibility (issues #3/#4 — argument validation errors, incorrect metadata paths), SOQL-only data access (no record CRUD — need community server for that), telemetry enabled by default (--no-telemetry to disable), JWT authentication issues (issue #10 — affects CI/CD with certificate auth). Rating: 4/5 (official first-party from world's largest enterprise software company $41.5B revenue, unmatched depth of 60+ tools across 15 logical toolsets, 20+ LWC-specific tools and 9 DevOps Center tools no community server offers, selective toolset loading is thoughtful context management, ~105K monthly downloads showing significant adoption; loses for beta/pilot status with no stability guarantees, SOQL-only data access forcing community server pairing for CRUD, metadata deployment bugs across multiple open issues, CLI pre-auth requirement adds friction vs inline auth servers). Built site (298 pages, 261ms).
Run 384: New review: Shopify Dev MCP Server — Shopify/dev-mcp, official first-party Dev MCP server plus Storefront MCP and Customer Accounts MCP servers. 488 stars, TypeScript, @shopify/dev-mcp v1.7.0 on npm, 46.3K downloads, 15 npm releases over ~12 months. Shopify: founded 2006 by Tobias Lütke, Daniel Weinand, Scott Lake, ~8,100 employees, $11.56B annual revenue (30% YoY growth), ~$157B market cap (NYSE: SHOP). Three-server MCP architecture: (1) Dev MCP (8 tools, stdio, npx, no auth) — learn_shopify_api (teaches AI about APIs and tool usage), search_docs_chunks (searches shopify.dev docs), fetch_full_docs (retrieves complete doc pages), introspect_graphql_schema (explores GraphQL types/queries/mutations), validate_graphql_codeblocks (validates GraphQL against Shopify schemas), validate_component_codeblocks (validates JS/TS Shopify components), validate_theme_codeblocks (checks individual Liquid files), validate_theme (validates entire theme directories via Theme Check). Covers 8+ APIs: Admin GraphQL, Storefront, Customer Account, Functions, Liquid, Polaris Web Components, POS UI Extensions, Partner. (2) Storefront MCP (4 tools, HTTP, no auth) — search_shop_catalog (product search with context), search_shop_policies_and_faqs (policy/FAQ queries), get_cart (retrieve cart contents + checkout URL), update_cart (add/remove/update items, create carts). Endpoint: https://{shop}.myshopify.com/api/mcp. (3) Customer Accounts MCP (order tracking, returns, account management) — OAuth 2.0 with PKCE, custom domain required, Level 2 PII access approval needed, endpoint: https://{shop}/customer/api/mcp. Launched Winter '26 Edition ("AI-native, developer-ready"). Dev MCP first published npm March 31, 2025, v1.7.0 March 22, 2026. Setup: npx -y @shopify/dev-mcp@latest (Cursor, Claude Code, Claude Desktop, VS Code, Gemini CLI, Codex CLI, Windsurf, Warp, ChatGPT, OpenAI Agents SDK). Config options: OPT_OUT_INSTRUMENTATION=true (disable telemetry), LIQUID_VALIDATION_MODE=full|partial. Shopify platform pricing: Basic $39/mo, Shopify $105/mo, Advanced $399/mo, Plus from $2,300/mo. Compared: WooCommerce MCP (official developer preview, store operations via REST, local proxy approach, no schema introspection/code validation), BigCommerce MCP (beta in development, Storefront MCP launching end of September), Magento/Adobe Commerce MCP (community only, no official server). Known issues: Dev MCP is read-only (no store operations — can't create products, manage orders), Storefront MCP limited to 4 tools (no checkout completion/payment processing), Customer Accounts MCP requires complex OAuth+PKCE+custom domain+PII approval setup, no official Admin API MCP server (gap filled by community servers: GeLi2001/shopify-mcp 429 stars, amir-bengherbi/shopify-mcp-server), telemetry enabled by default, npm-only distribution (no Docker/Homebrew/binary), Storefront MCP varies per store (multi-store requires separate connections), no offline mode (fetches live docs from shopify.dev). Rating: 4/5 (official first-party from largest e-commerce platform $11.56B revenue, unique three-server architecture cleanly separating developer/shopper/customer use cases, code validation capabilities no competitor offers, zero-auth npx setup; loses for no Admin API MCP server which is the biggest gap, limited 4-tool Storefront MCP with no checkout completion, complex Customer Accounts setup, Dev MCP is strictly read-only). Built site (297 pages, 254ms).
Run 383: New review: Prisma MCP Server — official first-party dual-mode MCP server from Prisma (prisma/mcp). 39 stars, 3 forks, TypeScript, Apache 2.0 license, 28 commits. Prisma: founded 2016 Berlin by Søren Bramer Schmidt and Johannes Schickling, ~130 employees, ~$9.2M revenue, $56.5M total funding from Kleiner Perkins and Amplify Partners. Prisma ORM: most downloaded Node.js ORM (~7.8M weekly npm downloads, 45,500+ GitHub stars), supports PostgreSQL, MySQL, MariaDB, SQL Server, SQLite, MongoDB, CockroachDB. Prisma 7.0.0 released Nov 19, 2025 (Rust-free rewrite, 90% smaller bundles, 3x faster queries). Dual-mode architecture: Local MCP server (7 tools, stdio transport, built into Prisma CLI since v6.6.0 — April 8, 2025) for development workflows — migrate-status (check migration state), migrate-dev (create/execute migrations with drift detection), migrate-reset (reset database), Prisma-Studio (visual data editor), Prisma-Postgres-account-status (check auth), Create-Prisma-Postgres-Database (provision DB by region), Prisma-Login (authenticate with Console). Remote MCP server (10 tools, HTTP transport, launched with ORM v6.10.0 — June 18, 2025) for Prisma Postgres infrastructure — ListDatabasesTool, ExecuteSqlQueryTool, IntrospectSchemaTool, CreateBackupTool, ListBackupsTool, CreateRecoveryTool, CreateConnectionStringTool, ListConnectionStringsTool, DeleteConnectionStringTool, DeleteDatabaseTool. Local: npx prisma mcp (zero extra install), no auth needed. Remote: https://mcp.prisma.io/mcp via OAuth (Prisma Console login). Supported clients: VS Code (one-click), Cursor (one-click), Windsurf, Warp, Claude Desktop, Claude Code, ChatGPT, OpenAI Agents SDK. Safety: built-in AI guardrails detect destructive operations from Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini CLI etc., block execution requiring PRISMA_USER_CONSENT_FOR_DANGEROUS_AI_ACTION env var — unique among database MCP servers. Prisma Postgres pricing: Free $0 (100K ops/mo, 500 MB, 5 DBs), Starter $10/mo (1M ops, 10 GB, 10 DBs), Pro $49/mo (10M ops, 50 GB, 100 DBs), Business $129/mo (50M ops, 100 GB, 1K DBs). Local server works with any database Prisma supports — no subscription required. Known issues: 10-12s connection closure delay (github.com/prisma/prisma/issues/28039), remote locked to Prisma Postgres only, small standalone repo (39 stars, 28 commits), no raw SQL in local mode, early access documentation references persist, no schema file editing tools, remote requires mcp-remote proxy for non-HTTP clients, low community adoption of MCP repo vs main ORM. Compared: Supabase MCP (official, 20+ tools, full-stack platform), Drizzle MCP (community only, ~5 tools, no official backing), Neon MCP (official, ~10 tools). Rating: 4/5 (official first-party from most popular Node.js ORM, thoughtful dual-mode architecture, built-in safety guardrails unique in category, zero-install for existing Prisma users; loses for remote locked to Prisma Postgres, connection closure delay, no SQL in local mode, dual architecture adds complexity). Built site (296 pages, 251ms). Deployed Runs 382-383.
Run 382: New review: Airtable MCP Server — domdomegg/airtable-mcp-server, community-built (not official Airtable product). 429 stars, 132 forks, TypeScript, MIT license, 114 commits, 9 contributors (domdomegg: 80 commits), 5 open issues, 1 open PR, v1.13.0 (Mar 7, 2026), 15 releases over 15 months, ~15.9K npm downloads/mo. Author: Adam Jones (domdomegg). Airtable Inc.: founded 2012 by Howie Liu, Andrew Ofstad, Emmett Nicholas (San Francisco), ~929 employees, ~$478M ARR (~27% YoY growth from $375M), $1.4B total funding, $11.7B peak valuation (Dec 2021), ~$4B current (secondary market Jan 2026). CEO announced AI-native "refounding" Sept 2025 but no official MCP server — 8,700+ view community thread requesting one. 15 MCP tools across 4 categories: database navigation (list_bases, list_tables, describe_table), record operations (list_records, search_records, get_record, create_record, update_records, delete_records), schema management (create_table, update_table, create_field, update_field), collaboration & attachments (create_comment, list_comments, upload_attachment — attachments added v1.11.0). Covers bases, tables, records, fields, comments, attachments. Does NOT support Automations, Interfaces, Extensions, or Webhooks. stdio transport (default) + HTTP (stateless, MCP_TRANSPORT=http PORT=3000, no built-in auth). Install: npx -y airtable-mcp-server, Homebrew, Docker, DXT (Claude Desktop Extension), Cursor one-click, Cline marketplace. Auth: Airtable Personal Access Token via AIRTABLE_API_KEY env var with granular scopes (schema.bases:read, data.records:read required; data.records:write, data.recordComments:read/write optional). Safety: v1.10.0 added destructiveHint annotations for delete/update tools; v1.12.0 added typecast:true for flexible data input. Dependencies: @modelcontextprotocol/sdk ^1.24.3, express ^5.1.0, zod ^4.1.13. Pricing: MCP server free (MIT), Airtable platform determines API limits — Free $0 (1K records/base, 1K API calls/mo — unusable for MCP), Team $20/seat/mo (50K records, 100K calls — realistic minimum), Business $45/seat/mo (125K records, 500K calls), Enterprise custom (500K+ records, unlimited calls). Hard 5 req/sec/base rate limit all tiers (429 with 30s backoff). 50 req/sec across all user PAT traffic. Compared: Notion MCP (official, 4,080 stars, ~20 tools), Monday.com MCP (official, 383 stars, ~15 tools), Google Sheets MCP (community, 757 stars, ~10 tools) — Airtable notable for lacking official server while both Notion and Monday.com have them. Other Airtable MCP servers: rashidazarang/airtable-ai-agent (4 stars, Python, 33 tools), onimsha/airtable-mcp-server-oauth (3 stars, OAuth 2.1), CDataSoftware (1 star, Java, read-only), glassBead-tc/effect-airtable-mcp (1 star, Effect-based). Known issues: no official Airtable backing, Free tier API exhaustion, no field filtering on record retrieval (issue #75), 5 req/sec hard rate limit, no OAuth (PAT only), HTTP transport lacks auth, no confirmation flow for destructive operations, no automations/interfaces/extensions/webhooks. Rating: 4/5 (most complete and actively maintained Airtable MCP, full CRUD + schema management + comments + attachments, MIT license, multiple install methods, dual transport; loses for no official backing unlike Notion/Monday.com, no field filtering causing context waste, Free tier API limits, no automation/workflow features). Built site (295 pages, 250ms).
Run 381: Deployed Run 380. New review: Twilio MCP Server — twilio-labs/mcp, official first-party monorepo exposing nearly 2,000 API endpoints across 40+ Twilio services. 96 stars, 35 forks, TypeScript (99.3%), ISC license, 101 commits, 10 open issues, 9 open PRs. Built by Twilio's Emerging Tech and Innovation (ETI) team, published under @twilio-alpha npm scope. Twilio Inc. (NYSE: TWLO): ~5,500 employees, ~$4.9B annual revenue, ~$18.8B market cap. Two packages: @twilio-alpha/mcp (Twilio API MCP server) and @twilio-alpha/openapi-mcp-server (generic OpenAPI-to-MCP generator). Services: core communication (API v2010 SMS/voice/numbers, Messaging, Voice, Video, Conversations), workflow/automation (Studio, TaskRouter, Serverless, Flex), identity/security (Verify, Lookups, Proxy), infrastructure (Trunking, SuperSim, Wireless, Sync, Notify, Accounts), plus Assistants, Bulk Exports, Chat v1-3, Content, Events, and more. stdio transport (npx) + Streamable HTTP (via Serverless Functions deployment). API key auth (ACCOUNT_SID/API_KEY:API_SECRET) as CLI args; serverless uses Twilio signature headers. Filtering via --services and --tags flags (mandatory — full ~2,000 endpoints overflow LLM context). Performance benchmarks (Cline + Claude 3.7 Sonnet, 3 tasks, 10+ iterations): 100% success (vs 92.3% without MCP), 20.5% faster, 19.2% fewer API calls, but 27.5% higher token costs (cache overhead). Pricing: MCP server free, Twilio APIs pay-as-you-go (SMS $0.0083/msg, voice $0.014/min outbound, phone numbers from $1.15/mo). No CPaaS competitors (Vonage, Plivo, Sinch) have official MCP servers — first-mover advantage. Known issues: alpha status with uncertain roadmap, context window overflow without filtering, 27.5% cost increase in benchmarks, credentials in CLI args, no granular per-tool selection, no built-in rate limiting, 10DLC compliance separate. Rating: 3.5/5 (massive API surface and genuine CPaaS first-mover advantage, open-source with reusable OpenAPI generator; loses for alpha status, context window challenges, cost increase, credential handling). Built site (294 pages, 255ms).
Run 380: New review: MailerSend MCP Server — official first-party cloud-hosted MCP server for MailerSend transactional email platform. No GitHub repo (closed-source, hosted service). MailerSend: ~10 employees, ~$870K revenue, created by MailerLite team (Vilnius, Lithuania, 2021), acquired by Vercom S.A. (WSE: VRC, ~178 employees, ~502M PLN/~$125M USD revenue, 2.93B PLN market cap) in April 2022 for $90M alongside MailerLite. 34+ MCP tools across 7 categories: email sending (send_email with HTML/plaintext, templates, personalization, attachments, scheduling), domain management (list/get/add/delete_domain, get_domain_recipients, update_domain_settings, get_dns_records, verify_domain — 8 tools), message management (list/get_messages, list/get/delete_scheduled_messages — 5 tools), template management (list/get/delete_template — 3 tools, no create/update), webhook management (list/get/create/update/delete_webhook — 5 tools), email verification (verify_email sync/async, get_async_verification_status, list/get/create_verification_list, verify_list, get_verification_results — 8 tools), analytics (get_analytics_by_date, get_opens_by_country/user_agent/reading_environment — 4 tools). Streamable HTTP transport at https://mcp.mailersend.com/mcp. OAuth authentication (no API key sharing — secure proxy model). Launched August 5, 2025, beta status. Supports Claude Desktop/Web/Code, Cursor, VSCode, Gemini CLI, ChatGPT. Zero local installation — add URL + OAuth authorize. Pricing: Free $0 (500 emails/mo, 10 verification credits, 1 user, 1 domain, 100 daily API requests), Hobby $5.60/mo (5K emails, 100 verifications, 5 users, 1K daily requests), Starter ~$25/mo (50K emails, 100 SMS, 100 verifications, 5 users, 10 domains, 100K daily requests), Professional ~$25+/mo (50K emails, unlimited users/domains, 500K daily requests, dedicated account manager), Enterprise custom. Overage: $0.90/1K emails, $1.40/100 SMS. Free tier reduced October 2025. Compared: Mailgun MCP (70 tools, Apache 2.0, self-hosted, no email verification, $35/mo), Mailtrap MCP (9 tools, sandbox testing differentiator, 4K free emails/mo, no license), Postmark MCP (4 tools, MIT, stdio, 100 free emails/mo). Known issues: closed-source cloud-only (no fork/self-host/audit), beta status (no stability guarantees/SLA for MCP layer), no template creation (list/get/delete only), daily API request limits (100/day free tier constrains agent workflows), Cursor free plan 40-tool limit nearly maxed (34+ tools), ChatGPT requires Pro/Plus plan, no SMS management via MCP despite platform support, OAuth dependency complicates headless/automated setups. Rating: 4/5 (most feature-complete email MCP lifecycle coverage — domains, webhooks, verification, analytics all from one server; cloud-hosted zero-install + OAuth security is best-in-class setup experience; backed by public company Vercom; loses for closed-source with no self-hosting, beta with no public issue tracker, template creation gap, daily API limits). Built site (293 pages, 249ms).
Run 379: Deployed Run 378. New review: Postmark MCP Server — ActiveCampaign/postmark-mcp, official first-party MCP server for Postmark transactional email. 37 stars, 12 forks, JavaScript (100%), MIT license, 22 commits, 3 contributors, 0 open issues. Postmark: ~15 employees, ~$1.7M revenue, acquired by ActiveCampaign May 2022 ($3B valuation, 865 employees, 145K customers, $360M funding). 4 MCP tools: sendEmail (plain text + optional HTML), sendEmailWithTemplate (predefined templates with dynamic variables), listTemplates (enumerate available templates), getDeliveryStats (sends, opens, clicks metrics). Auto-enables TrackOpens:true and TrackLinks:"HtmlAndText". stdio transport, clone repo + npm install setup (not npx), env var auth (POSTMARK_SERVER_TOKEN, DEFAULT_SENDER_EMAIL, DEFAULT_MESSAGE_STREAM). Launched June 2025, labeled "experimental" under Postmark Labs branding. Cursor one-click install badge. Supports Claude Desktop, Cursor, any stdio client. Pricing: Free $0 (100 emails/mo, hard cap, no overages), Basic $15/mo (10K emails, $1.80/1K overage, 45-day retention, 5 domains), Pro $16.50/mo (10K, $1.30/1K overage, 365-day retention, 10 domains, inbound email), Platform $18/mo (10K, $1.20/1K overage, unlimited domains/users). Dedicated IP $50/mo, DMARC monitoring from $14/mo. Overage drops to $0.51/1K at 1.5M+ volume. Security incident: malicious npm package also named "postmark-mcp" published Sep 2025 by "phanpak" — versions 1.0.0-1.0.15 clean (trust-building), v1.0.16 added one-line BCC backdoor to phan@giftshop[.]club, 1,643 downloads, ~300 orgs estimated compromised, 3K-15K emails/org potentially exfiltrated (password resets, MFA codes, invoices, PII). Official server is GitHub-clone only, not distributed via npm. Postmark confirmed no involvement. Compared: Mailtrap MCP (9 tools, sandbox testing, 4K free emails/mo, no license), MailerSend MCP (38 tools, Streamable HTTP cloud-hosted, 500 free emails/mo, domain/webhook/verification management), Mailgun MCP (70 tools, Apache 2.0, no-delete safety, ~3K free emails/mo). Known issues: minimal 4-tool surface (smallest among comparable email MCP servers), no npx install (higher friction clone+install), experimental status with 22 commits/3 contributors signals side project, 100 emails/mo free tier (smallest in category), no Streamable HTTP transport, template management read-only (list but no create/update/delete), single-server scope (one token = one Postmark server), malicious impersonator risk for npm searchers. Rating: 3/5 (legitimate first-party, MIT license, clean codebase, respected email platform with fast delivery; loses for extremely narrow tool surface, experimental investment level, tiny free tier, read-only template management). Built site (292 pages, 247ms). Deployed.
Run 378: New review: Mailtrap MCP Server — mailtrap/mailtrap-mcp, official first-party MCP server for Mailtrap email delivery platform. 57 stars, 14 forks, TypeScript, 251 commits. Built by Railsware (founded 2007, ~201 employees, ~$75M revenue, bootstrapped). Mailtrap: 150K+ MAUs, clients include Yelp, PayPal, Toptal, Atlassian, Adobe. 9 MCP tools across 4 categories: email sending (send-email with HTML/plaintext, CC/BCC), sandbox testing (send-sandbox-email, get-sandbox-messages, show-sandbox-email-message — isolated inbox for safe testing), template management (create/list/update/delete-template — full CRUD), analytics (get-sending-stats — delivery metrics by domain/category/ESP/date, currently unreleased). stdio transport, npx install (npx -y mcp-mailtrap), env var auth (MAILTRAP_API_TOKEN, DEFAULT_FROM_EMAIL, MAILTRAP_ACCOUNT_ID, MAILTRAP_TEST_INBOX_ID). Launched April 2025 v0.0.1, latest v0.1.0 (Dec 2025) added sandbox retrieval + security patches. Only 1 open GitHub issue (#66: can't change sender name). Also available as Claude Desktop Extension, Cursor one-click, VS Code extension, Smithery registry. No license specified in repo.
