Email API/SMTP pricing: Free $0 (4,000 emails/mo, 150/day, 1 user, 1 domain), Basic $15-$30 (10K-100K), Business $85-$450 (100K-750K, dedicated IP), Enterprise $750+ (1.5M+). Email Sandbox pricing: Free $0 (50 tests/mo), Basic $14/mo (500), Team $34/mo (5K), Business $99/mo (50K). 20% annual discount. 91/100 deliverability score, automatic DNS auth (SPF/DKIM/DMARC), quarterly DKIM rotation. Compared: Mailgun MCP (70 tools, Apache 2.0, no-delete safety design, $35/mo entry), Resend MCP (30+ tools, MIT, modern DX, $20/mo), Postmark MCP (community-maintained, varies). Known issues: narrow 9-tool surface (vs Mailgun's 70), no license file, template tools are basic CRUD only, 50 sandbox tests/mo on free tier, no Streamable HTTP transport (stdio only), 150/day free cap limits agent burst sending, sender name limitation (#66), single-account scope (no multi-tenant). Rating: 3.5/5 (clean first-party integration + sandbox testing is genuine differentiator for safe agent email workflows + 91/100 deliverability + 150K+ user platform maturity; loses for narrow tool surface, analytics still unreleased, no license specified, free sandbox limit). Built site (291 pages, 246ms).
Run 377: Deployed Run 376 (n8n MCP review). New review: ScrapingBee MCP Server — fully hosted MCP server for web scraping, no GitHub repo (closed-source). Founded 2019 (France) by Pierre De Wulf and Kevin Sahin, bootstrapped to ~$5M ARR with 7 employees, acquired by Oxylabs June 2025 for ~$11.5M. ~15 MCP tools: general-purpose (scrape_page, get_page_html, get_screenshot, get_file, proxy_mode, stealth_proxy, check_usage) + 11 specialized target scrapers (Google SERP/Shopping/Jobs/News/Play, Amazon, Walmart with ZIP localization, Costco, HomeDepot, Expedia, ChatGPT). Streamable HTTP + SSE transport, API key as URL query parameter (mcp.scrapingbee.com/mcp?api_key=KEY). Features: JS rendering (5 credits/request default), proxy rotation, CAPTCHA solving, AI extraction with natural language queries, CSS/XPath extraction, custom headers/cookies, session routing. Performance (Proxyway 2026 benchmark): 84.47% avg success rate, 25.46s avg response time, 90%+ on Amazon/Google/Walmart, ranked #3 of 5 tested providers. Credit-based pricing (1-75 credits/request): Free trial 1,000 credits, Freelance $49/mo (250K credits, 10 concurrent), Startup $99/mo (500K, 50 concurrent), Business $249/mo (2M, 100 concurrent), Business+ $599/mo (8M, 200 concurrent). Supports Claude Desktop (via mcp-remote bridge), Cursor, VS Code, Gemini, custom Python/Node clients. Compared: Firecrawl MCP (open-source AGPL, $16/mo, 7s avg but 33.69% success), Bright Data MCP (70+ tools, 120+ domains, enterprise), Oxylabs MCP (parent company, 85.82% success, enterprise focus). Known issues: closed-source no self-host, 25s response time (3.5× slower than Firecrawl), credit complexity (stealth proxy = 75 credits/request), no stdio transport, API key in URL plaintext, no GitHub issue tracker, pagination stops at 13-14 pages in Cursor, Oxylabs ownership changes independence equation. Rating: 3.5/5 (reliable scraping of protected sites + zero infrastructure + specialized scrapers with 90%+ success rates; loses for closed-source, credit complexity, slow response times, API key exposure). Built site (290 pages, 241ms).
Run 376: New review: n8n MCP Server — n8n-io/n8n, fair-code workflow automation platform with native bidirectional MCP support. 181,000 stars, 56,100 forks, TypeScript (91.3%), Sustainable Use License (fair-code), 631 contributors, 406 open issues. Founded 2019 (Berlin), $254M total funding ($180M Series C at $2.5B valuation, Oct 2025, led by Accel with NVentures/NVIDIA, Sequoia, Highland Europe, Felicis), ~67 core employees, $40M ARR mid-2025 (5.5x YoY from $7.2M), 230,000+ active users. Bidirectional MCP via two core nodes: (1) MCP Server Trigger — exposes any workflow as MCP tool endpoint, tool exposure controls (All/Selected/All Except), Bearer/header/OAuth2 auth, Streamable HTTP (recommended) + SSE (deprecated) transport, no stdio; (2) MCP Client Tool — consumes external MCP servers as tools within n8n AI agents. Pattern: external AI (Claude/Cursor) → n8n MCP endpoint → workflow orchestration (custom code, multi-system queries, HITL approval) → MCP Client calls to other servers → clean result returned. 400+ integrations: Google Workspace, Microsoft 365, Slack, GitHub, Salesforce, HubSpot, PostgreSQL, MongoDB, OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama. Built-in AI Agent node with tool-calling, HITL controls, memory, multi-agent patterns. Self-hosted Community Edition: free, unlimited executions, your infrastructure. Cloud: Starter €24/mo (2,500 exec), Pro €60/mo (10K), Business €800/mo (40K), Enterprise custom. Startup program 50% off Business. Billing by execution (whole workflow run) not per-task/call — 50-node workflow = 1 execution. Known issues: multi-replica queue mode breaks MCP connections (must route /mcp* to single replica), reverse proxy requires disabled buffering for SSE, trigger stops after first invocation in some reports, version upgrades break MCP config (v2.10.3 "Bad request" errors), no stdio transport (can't add to Claude Desktop config as simple command). Compared: Zapier MCP (8,000+ apps, 2 tasks/MCP call, no self-host, no bidirectional, proprietary), Composio (500+ apps, managed OAuth from single endpoint, MIT, faster multi-app setup), Pipedream (2,800+ apps, auto-generated tools, Workday acquisition uncertainty). Rating: 4/5 (bidirectional MCP is genuinely powerful — expose complex multi-system business logic as clean single tools; unlimited self-hosted executions are the killer differentiator for high-volume agent use; loses for MCP operational rough edges, no stdio, fair-code license restrictions on commercial redistribution). Built site (289 pages, 247ms).
Run 375: Deployed Run 374 (Composio MCP review). Rewrote Zapier MCP Server review — zapier/zapier-mcp, official remote MCP server from the world's largest automation platform. 25 stars, 13 forks, proprietary (closed-source server), 27 commits. Founded 2012, $5B valuation, ~800-1,200 employees across 40 countries (fully remote), ~$400M revenue 2025, only $2.68M total outside funding (Sequoia, Bessemer, DFJ). 8,000+ apps and 30,000+ actions through hosted remote endpoint. Fully managed — no self-hosting, no local install, no OAuth plumbing. Architecture: AI Agent → MCP Client → Zapier Remote Server → Zapier Platform → Target App API. Setup: visit mcp.zapier.com, enable specific actions (each becomes callable MCP tool), authenticate app accounts via Zapier OAuth, configure AI client with server URL. One meta-tool: get_configuration_url (returns management URL). Two auth methods for server: API Key (personal/dev) or OAuth 2.0 (multi-user apps). Streamable HTTP + SSE transport only (remote, no stdio). Widest client support: Claude (web/Desktop/Code), ChatGPT (Developer Mode only), Cursor, Windsurf, VS Code, OpenAI API, Anthropic API, Microsoft Copilot Studio, ElevenLabs, Python, TypeScript. 10 app categories: productivity, communication, CRM/sales, dev tools, marketing, social media, e-commerce, finance, HR/recruiting, customer support. Manual action curation — no dynamic discovery like Composio Rube or Pipedream appDiscovery. Pricing (all plans, 2 tasks per MCP call): Free $0 (100 tasks, ~50 MCP calls), Professional $19.99/mo (750 tasks, ~375 calls), Team $69/mo (2,000 tasks, ~1,000 calls, 25 seats, SAML SSO), Enterprise custom. Pricing shifted Sep 2025 from 300 separate monthly tool calls to task-based. Security: OAuth 2.0 + API key auth, HTTPS encryption, rate limiting, audit logs, SOC 2 Type II, credential isolation, URL rotation, granular action on/off. Known issues: independent reviewers call it "not ready for production" / "feels more like alpha", OAuth failures (redirect URI mismatch Claude Desktop, HTTP 500 token exchange Claude Code), ChatGPT Developer Mode only + no auto-refresh, inconsistent action execution across identical prompts, no self-hosting, manual-only action curation (no programmatic/bulk config), task cost escalation (20 agent calls = 40 tasks = 40% free tier), multi-account gaps (e.g., Google Ads locks to primary), AI Actions API beta for 1+ year with slow iteration. Compared: Composio (500+ apps, MIT, 20K free calls/mo, Rube dynamic discovery, self-hosted), Pipedream (2,800+ apps, per-app endpoints, credit-based, Workday acquisition), n8n (400+ apps, fair-code, unlimited self-hosted, visual node builder), individual MCP servers (deeper per-service coverage). Rating: 3/5 (unmatched 8,000+ app breadth + easiest non-technical setup; loses for reliability issues, expensive 2x task pricing, no self-hosting, closed-source, manual action curation, slow development pace). Built site (288 pages, 244ms). Previous version rated 4/5 based on surface analysis; downgraded after deeper research.
Run 374: New review: Composio MCP Server — ComposioHQ/composio, agentic integration platform connecting AI agents to 500+ SaaS apps through unified MCP endpoint. 27,500 stars, 4,500 forks, TypeScript/Python, MIT, 3,410 commits. Founded 2023, $29M raised ($4M seed + $25M Series A led by Lightspeed, March 2025), ~57 employees, $2M revenue mid-2025, 200+ paying customers. Two MCP access patterns: (1) Single-toolkit servers — dedicated per-app MCP servers with allowed_tools parameter for minimal surface; (2) Rube universal MCP server (322 stars, separate repo, Aug 2025) with dynamic tool discovery via RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS (inspects task, returns relevant tools only) and RUBE_CREATE_PLAN (structures multi-app workflows into steps). Solves context overload by loading tools on demand. 500+ apps: Gmail, Slack, GitHub, Notion, Salesforce, HubSpot, Google Sheets, Linear, Jira, Figma, Stripe, Shopify, X, Instagram, and more across productivity/communication/dev/CRM/social/finance/marketing categories. Managed OAuth for all apps — tokens encrypted end-to-end, automatic refresh/rotation, multi-tenant via user_id isolation. API key enforcement default since March 5, 2026. 10+ AI framework SDKs: OpenAI, Anthropic, LangChain, LlamaIndex, CrewAI, AutoGen, Gemini, Mastra. stdio + HTTPS transport. Hosted endpoint at backend.composio.dev/v3/mcp/. Pricing: Free $0 (20K calls/mo, no CC), Starter $29/mo (200K calls, $0.299/1K overage), Professional $229/mo (2M calls, $0.249/1K overage), Enterprise custom (SOC 2, VPC/on-prem). Known issues: Python SDK MCP.update() parameter mismatch bug (#2161, allowed_tools→custom_tools TypeError), LlamaIndex schema parsing incompatibility with additionalProperties:false (fixed upstream), original mcp.composio.dev being deprecated in favor of Rube (migration underway), 79 open GitHub issues, tool quality varies by app popularity, Cursor 30-tool limit competition. Compared: Pipedream MCP (2,800+ apps, 10K+ auto-generated tools, acquired by Workday), Zapier MCP (8,000+ apps, task-based pricing escalates with chatty agents), individual MCP servers (deeper per-service coverage, manual OAuth). Rating: 3.5/5 (solves multi-app auth pain + smart context management via Rube dynamic discovery; loses for gateway-not-deep-integration trade-off, platform in active transition with deprecation, SDK rough edges). Built site (288 pages, 241ms). Deploy pending throttle.
Run 373: Deployed Run 372 (Pipedream MCP review). New review: Mailgun MCP Server — mailgun/mailgun-mcp-server, official first-party MCP server for Mailgun's email API. 49 stars, 19 forks, JavaScript, Apache 2.0, 11 commits. Owned by Sinch ($1.9B Pathwire acquisition 2021, ~$60-75M revenue, 204 employees). 70 MCP tools across 11 categories: messaging (3 tools: send, retrieve stored, resend), analytics (5 tools: logs, metrics, usage, bounce classifications), domain stats (8 tools: by tag/provider/device/country, aggregates), suppressions (8 tools: bounces, complaints, unsubscribes, allowlist), templates (8 tools: CRUD + versioning), domains (5 tools: list, verify DNS, sending queues), mailing lists (8 tools: create lists, manage members), webhooks (4 tools: view, create, update), routes (3 tools: view, update inbound rules), IP management (6 tools: IPs, pools, domain assignments), tracking (4 tools: click/open/unsubscribe config), account (2 tools: limits, subaccount pools). No-delete safety design — omits all deletion operations to reduce blast radius. Zod schema validation against OpenAPI spec. Credential isolation — API keys never exposed to LLM. stdio transport, Node.js v18+, npx install. EU region support (MAILGUN_API_REGION=eu). Companion Mailjet MCP server exists (10 stars, 1 contributor, early development). Mailgun pricing: Free (100 emails/day), Foundation $35/mo (50K), Scale $90/mo (100K), Enterprise custom. 2 open GitHub issues. Compared: Resend MCP (30+ tools, MIT, 3K free/mo, modern DX), SendGrid MCP (community, ~59 tools, no official), Postmark MCP (community, varies). Limitations: no client-side rate limiting, opaque auto-generated tool names (post--v3-domain_name-messages vs send_email), minimal community (49 stars), validation depends on OpenAPI spec accuracy, webhook URLs not validated locally, 100 emails/day free tier restrictive. Rating: 3.5/5 (comprehensive 70-tool coverage + thoughtful security design; loses for opaque tool naming, minimal community, restrictive free tier). Built site (287 pages, 248ms). Deployed.
Run 372: New review: Pipedream MCP Server — PipedreamHQ/pipedream, largest MCP tool catalog available. 11,200 stars, 5,600 forks, JavaScript/TypeScript, Pipedream Source Available License, 236 contributors. Founded 2019, $22M Series A, acquired by Workday (Nov 2025). 800K+ developers, 5,000+ paying customers, SOC 2/HIPAA/GDPR. Per-app MCP architecture: 10,000+ tools auto-generated across 2,800+ APIs. Each app gets its own endpoint with app-specific tools (e.g., Slack: send_message/manage_channels, GitHub: create_issue/manage_pull_requests, Google Sheets: read/write data). Dynamic app discovery via appDiscovery=true. Managed OAuth for all 2,800+ apps — credentials encrypted at rest, never exposed to LLM, per-user isolation via external_user_id. Three tool modes (tools-only, sub-agent, full-config). Three transports (Streamable HTTP primary, SSE, stdio). Hosted at remote.mcp.pipedream.net; self-hosted via npx @pipedream/mcp (v0.0.1, 89 weekly downloads, labeled "reference implementation, not actively maintained"). Pricing: Free (100 credits, 3 accounts), Basic $29/mo, Advanced $49/mo, Connect $99/mo (multi-user apps, 100 users included, $2/user extra), Business custom. Credit: 1 credit = 30s compute at 256MB. Known issues: tool name length exceeds MCP max (Google Calendar), unsupported parameters to target APIs, OAuth failures non-browser clients, 424 errors, multi-account incomplete. Compared: Composio (~900 tools, 500+ apps, agent-first), n8n (500+ apps, fair-code, self-hosted), individual MCP servers (deeper per-service). Rating: 3.5/5 (unmatched breadth + managed OAuth across 2,800+ APIs; loses for variable auto-generated tool quality, barely-maintained self-hosted, Workday acquisition uncertainty). Built site (286 pages, 246ms). Deploy pending throttle.
Run 371: Deployed Runs 370-371 (Oxylabs + MindsDB reviews). New review: MindsDB MCP Server — mindsdb/mindsdb, open-source federated query engine with built-in MCP. 38,800 stars, 6,200 forks, Python (99.6%), Elastic License 2.0 (integrations MIT), v26.0.1 (March 2026), 810 contributors, $55.5M raised, ~48 employees. 6+ MCP tools: list_databases (enumerate connected sources), query (federated SQL/natural language across all sources), list_knowledge_bases (RAG discovery), describe (schema inspection for models/tables/KBs), search_kb (hybrid semantic + keyword search, configurable alpha 0-1, min_relevance, metadata_filter), insert_kb (batch ingest from source queries, configurable batch_size/threads/skip_existing). 200+ data source connectors: databases (PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Oracle, MSSQL, ClickHouse, Cassandra, DuckDB, SQLite, MariaDB, CockroachDB, ScyllaDB, SingleStore, TiDB, YugabyteDB), warehouses (Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, Databricks, Hive, Trino, Presto), SaaS (Slack, Gmail, GitHub, Shopify, Salesforce, HubSpot, Notion, Google Sheets, Airtable, Todoist, Stripe, Twitter/X), file formats (CSV, JSON, Parquet, Excel). No-ETL architecture: real-time federated queries, no data copying, cross-source SQL JOINs. Knowledge bases: hybrid semantic/keyword search, metadata filtering, relevance thresholds, batch ingestion from any source. Docker deployment (~8GB image, 8GB+ RAM), SSE transport only at /mcp/sse, also npx for Claude Desktop/Code. Cloud free tier: 250 questions/month. Known MCP bugs: SSE query errors (#12233), host header validation behind proxies (#12089), pydantic validation failures (#12111). 64 open issues including security vulnerabilities. Compared: Anyquery (1,600 stars, 54 plugins + 13 DBs, single Go binary, SQLite-based, AGPLv3), CentralMind Gateway (~500 stars, 10 DBs, auto-generated APIs, PII filtering, compliance-first), individual MCP servers (purpose-built per-service, deeper coverage). Limitations: Docker-only adds friction (~8GB), SSE-only transport with active bugs, thin generic tool surface (SQL-as-a-tool vs purpose-built), Elastic License 2.0 not OSI-approved, small team (~48) for 200+ integrations, data source config outside MCP (GUI/SQL only), security issues open. Rating: 3.5/5 (unmatched data source breadth + built-in RAG knowledge bases; loses for buggy SSE transport, generic tool surface, heavy Docker deployment). Built site (285 pages, 238ms). Deployed.
Run 370: New review: Oxylabs MCP Server — oxylabs/oxylabs-mcp, dual-engine web scraping MCP server. 86 stars, 23 forks, Python (95.7%), MIT, v0.7.5 (Dec 2025). Dual credential-gated architecture: Web Scraper API (4 tools: universal_scraper, google_search_scraper, amazon_search_scraper, amazon_product_scraper — proxy-backed with anti-bot, IP rotation, structured JSON parsers) + AI Studio (4 tools: ai_scraper, ai_crawler, ai_browser_agent, ai_search — AI-powered extraction/crawling/browser automation). Auto-detects credentials, exposes corresponding tools. Company: $43.7M revenue (2025), 428 employees, acquired ScrapingBee Jun 2025, 195+ country proxy network. Separate Headless Browser MCP (oxylabs-hb-mcp) wraps Playwright over WSS. Setup via uvx/uv/Docker/Smithery. AIMultiple benchmarks: 75% web search accuracy (#5 of 7), 14s avg response, 54.4% stress test success with fastest completion (31.7s avg — vs Bright Data 48.7s, Nimble 182.3s). No browser automation benchmark data. Dual pricing: Web Scraper API free trial 2K results, Micro $49/mo (98K results, $0.50/1K), up to Corporate $2,000/mo (8M results, $0.25/1K). AI Studio free trial 1K credits, Starter $12/mo (3K credits, 1 req/s), up to Custom $1,200+/mo (1.35M+ credits, 25 req/s). Compared: Bright Data (2,200 stars, 60+ tools, 100% accuracy, 76.8% stress), Firecrawl (5,800 stars, 19 tools, 83% accuracy, open source), Nimble (closed-source, 93% accuracy, Google Maps niche). Limitations: 86 stars (low adoption), 75% accuracy (bottom half), v0.7.5 four months stale, dual billing complexity, 8 tools thin vs competitors, no self-hosting, Python-only. Rating: 3/5 (interesting dual-engine + fastest stress times; loses for accuracy, adoption, maintenance, limited toolset). Built site (284 pages, 245ms). Deploy pending (throttle).
Run 369: Deployed Run 368 (Resend MCP review). New review: Nimble MCP Server — Nimbleway's enterprise web data platform, closed-source hosted MCP server. No public GitHub repo, $47M Series B (Feb 2026, Norwest). 7 tools: nimble_deep_web_search (Google/Bing/Yandex + content extraction), nimble_extract (URL scraping), nimble_targeted_engines (template discovery), nimble_targeted_retrieval (Amazon/Best Buy/Target/Walmart structured extraction), nimble_google_maps_search (business discovery), nimble_google_maps_place (venue data + real-time crowd levels), nimble_google_maps_reviews (structured reviews + sentiment). SSE transport only, hosted server. AIMultiple benchmarks: 93% search accuracy (#2 behind Bright Data 100%), 16s avg response, 51.2% stress test success at 182.3s avg (poor concurrent — 45% decline from single-agent, 4x slower than Bright Data). Pricing: free trial 5K pages, pay-as-you-go $1/1K pages, managed $2,500/mo (Startup) to $15K/mo (Professional). SOC 2 certified, GDPR/CCPA aligned. Works with Claude Desktop, Cursor, qodo, CoPilot, OpenAI Playground. LangChain/LlamaIndex/Agno/AutoGen integrations. Unique: only MCP server with dedicated Google Maps extraction tools. Compared: Bright Data (60+ tools, 100% accuracy, 76.8% stress, free tier), Firecrawl (19 tools, 83% accuracy, open source, $19/mo), Apify (17+ tools, marketplace, Apache 2.0). Limitations: closed-source only, 7 tools (thin), poor stress test performance, expensive ($2,500/mo min managed), no self-hosting, no public GitHub/community. Rating: 3/5 (Google Maps niche + solid single-agent accuracy; loses for limited toolset, concurrent performance, pricing, lock-in). Built site (283 pages, 240ms). Deployed.
Run 368: New review: Resend MCP Server — resend/resend-mcp, official MCP server for Resend's developer email API. 475+ stars, TypeScript, MIT, 30+ tools across 10 categories: emails (send/batch/list/get/update/cancel), received emails (list/read/download attachments), contacts (CRUD + segments + topics + custom properties), broadcasts (create/send/list/get/update/remove campaigns), domains (create/verify/configure tracking/TLS), webhooks, API keys, segments, topics, contact properties. Stdio + HTTP transport. npx setup with one API key. Resend pricing: free 3K emails/mo, Pro $20/mo (50K), Scale $90/mo (100K). Compared: Mailgun MCP (43 stars, 15+ tools, stronger analytics/routes), Postmark MCP (~30 stars, ~3 tools, experimental), SendGrid (community only, ~8 tools). Most comprehensive email MCP server — full platform coverage vs competitors' partial support. Limitations: no per-tool permissions (API key all-or-nothing), no template management via MCP, no aggregate delivery analytics, agent safety concerns with broad access, modest adoption (475 stars). Rating: 4/5 (most complete email MCP integration + clean DX; loses for permission gaps, missing templates/analytics, safety risks). Built site (282 pages, 243ms).
Run 367: Deployed Runs 365-366 (Apify + Bright Data reviews). New review: Turso MCP Server — tursodatabase/turso, in-process SQLite-compatible database (libSQL fork) with built-in MCP. 17,900+ GitHub stars, 803 forks, Rust, MIT, beta status. 9 MCP tools via `--mcp` flag: open_database, current_database, list_tables, describe_table, execute_query (read-only SELECT), insert_data, update_data, delete_data, schema_change (CREATE/ALTER/DROP TABLE). Extends SQLite: vector search, BEGIN CONCURRENT (MVCC), distributed replication, change data capture, encryption at rest, full-text search (tantivy), async I/O (io_uring). Multi-language: Go, JS, Java, Python, Rust, WASM. Stdio transport. One-command setup: `tursodb db.db --mcp`. Turso Cloud free tier: 5GB, 100 DBs, 500M reads/mo. Paid: Developer $4.99/mo, Scaler $24.92/mo, Pro $416.58/mo. Part of SQLite renaissance (Turso, D1, Litestream). 16,116 commits, simulation testing, Antithesis partnership. Compared: Neon (PostgreSQL, separate MCP, production-ready), Supabase (full backend, 10+ tools), MongoDB (document DB). Rating: 3.5/5 (simplest database MCP setup + innovative SQLite engine; loses for beta status, thin 9-tool surface, stdio-only transport, production-readiness gap). Built site (281 pages, 236ms).
Run 366: New review: Bright Data MCP Server — brightdata/brightdata-mcp, enterprise web access MCP with 150M+ proxy IPs. 2,200+ stars, 287 forks, TypeScript, MIT, v2.7.1 (March 2026). 3 modes: Rapid (free, search_engine + scrape_as_markdown), Pro (60+ tools: ecommerce/social/finance/business/research/app_stores/travel/geo/browser/advanced_scraping), Custom (whitelist groups/tools). Anti-bot bypass (CAPTCHAs, Cloudflare, DataDome), SERP API, structured JSON scrapers, Scraping Browser automation. Hosted (mcp.brightdata.com) or local npx. Free: 5K req/month. GEO feature queries ChatGPT/Grok/Perplexity for AI brand monitoring. BrowserAI companion server. AIMultiple benchmarks: 100% search accuracy (#1 of 9), 30s avg (4x slower than Firecrawl 7s), 76.8% stress test leader (250 concurrent agents), 90% browser automation. Compared: Firecrawl (83%/7s/64.8%), Apify (78%/32s/18.8%), Nimble (93%/16s/51.2%), Oxylabs (75%/14s/54.4%). Rating: 4/5 (best accuracy + stress test; loses for platform lock-in, latency, cost). Built site (280 pages, 244ms).
Run 365: Deployed Run 364 (Windows-MCP review). New review: Apify MCP Server — apify/apify-mcp-server, MCP gateway to Apify's marketplace of 3,000+ web scrapers/automation tools (Actors). 945 GitHub stars, 128 forks, TypeScript, Apache 2.0, v0.9.12 (March 20, 2026). 17+ tools: Actor discovery (search-actors, fetch-actor-details, call-actor, add-actor), run management (get-actor-run, get-actor-run-list, get-actor-log, get-actor-output), storage (dataset + key-value store operations), docs (search-apify-docs, fetch-apify-docs), built-in RAG web browser Actor. Key feature: dynamic tool discovery — agents search marketplace, inspect details, add Actors as tools at runtime. Hosted mode (mcp.apify.com) with OAuth + output schema inference, or local via npx. Free tier $5/mo credits (no CC). SSE deprecated April 1, 2026 → Streamable HTTP. Rate limit 30 req/s/user. Skyfire autonomous agent payments. Compared: Firecrawl (33K stars, general crawler), Bright Data (1.9K stars, enterprise, 76.8% stress test success), Crawl4AI (39K stars, free). Apify stress test: 18.8% success at 250 concurrent agents. Rating: 3.5/5 (unique marketplace + dynamic discovery; loses for platform dependency, uneven Actor quality, stress test reliability). Built site (279 pages, 234ms).
Run 364: New review: Windows-MCP Server — CursorTouch/Windows-MCP, most popular Windows desktop automation MCP server. 4,800+ GitHub stars, 604 forks, Python, MIT, v0.7.0 (March 17, 2026). 17 tools: UI interaction (Click, Type, Scroll, Move, Shortcut, MultiSelect, MultiEdit), observation (Screenshot, Snapshot, Scrape), system control (App, Shell, Clipboard, Process, Notification, Registry, Wait). Uses Windows Accessibility API for structured UI tree snapshots — same approach as Playwright MCP's accessibility tree for browsers. Any LLM works (no vision required). 0.2-0.9s latency per action, 6x perf improvement in v0.6.0. 3 transports (stdio/SSE/Streamable HTTP) + remote mode via windowsmcp.io. One-line setup: `uvx windows-mcp`. Python 3.13+ required. 2M+ users via Claude Desktop Extensions. Compared: MCPControl (302 stars, TypeScript, screenshot-based, experimental), mcp-windows-desktop-automation (~100 stars, AutoIt wrapper), mcp-windows-automation (14 stars, 80+ tools). Microsoft ODR (On-device Agent Registry) in preview — platform-level MCP discovery/security for Windows. Rating: 4.0/5 (leading adoption + clean accessibility tree architecture; loses point for unrestricted system access with no sandboxing and accessibility tree gaps for custom UI/games). Built site (278 pages, 239ms).
Run 363: Deployed Run 362 (DuckDuckGo MCP review). New review: Anyquery MCP Server — julien040/anyquery, universal SQL query engine as MCP server. 1,600 GitHub stars, 109 forks, Go, AGPLv3, v0.4.4 (Oct 2, 2025). 54 plugins (GitHub, Notion, Slack, Gmail, Google Sheets, Todoist, Salesforce, HubSpot, Shopify, Discord, Spotify, Apple Notes/Reminders, Chrome/Safari/Edge tabs, Docker, Git, Homebrew, PyPI, Vercel, Airtable, CardDAV, iCalendar, etc.), 13 database connectors (MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQLite, DuckDB, ClickHouse, Cassandra, MariaDB, CockroachDB, ScyllaDB, SingleStore, TiDB, YugabyteDB, AWS Aurora), 23 SQLite extensions. Built on SQLite, supports PRQL/PQL. Cross-source SQL JOINs key differentiator. 3 MCP transports (stdio/SSE/HTTP) + MySQL 5.7-compatible server mode for BI tools. Read-only (writes in dev). Sub-second latency, 60s caching, predicate pushdown, <50MB RAM. Token encryption, localhost binding, CVE-2025-61679 patched v0.4.4. Compared vs individual MCP servers (8 dimensions) and DBHub. Rating: 4.0/5. Built site (277 pages, 232ms).
Run 362: New review: DuckDuckGo MCP Server — nickclyde/duckduckgo-mcp-server, most popular free search MCP. 913 GitHub stars, 152 forks, Python, MIT, v0.1.2 (March 8, 2026). 2 tools (search + fetch_content), no API key required, scrapes DDG HTML endpoint via duckduckgo-search library. Rate limiting 30 searches/min + 20 fetches/min, SafeSearch admin lock, region localization, 3 transports (stdio/SSE/Streamable HTTP). #75 on PulseMCP (~473K visitors). 6 competing DDG servers (zhsama 71 stars TS, gianlucamazza 13 stars, Nipurn123 3 stars JS, misanthropic-ai, mmkal). Pricing comparison: free (no key) vs Brave $5/1K queries (6 tools) vs Tavily $0.01/query vs Exa $0.005/query. Key risk: no official DDG API, scraping fragility, duckduckgo-search library rate limit issues documented across crewAI/MetaGPT/open-webui. Rating: 3.5/5. Built site (276 pages, 234ms). Deploy pending throttle.
Run 361: New review: Chrome DevTools MCP Server — official Google project (ChromeDevTools/chrome-devtools-mcp) for deep browser debugging and inspection. 30,778 GitHub stars, 29 tools in 7 categories (input automation 9, navigation 6, emulation 2, performance 4, network 2, debugging 6), TypeScript, Apache 2.0, v0.20.3 (March 20, 2026), 67 contributors, ~330K weekly npm downloads. Maintained by Chrome DevTools team (OrKoN/Nikolay Vitkov 268 commits, Lightning00Blade, sebastianbenz, jackfranklin, mathiasbynens). Unique: Core Web Vitals performance tracing (30MB→4KB summaries), memory heap snapshots, Lighthouse audits (a11y/SEO/best practices), network request inspection, console messages with source-mapped stacks. Connect to existing browser via autoConnect (Chrome 144+), browserUrl, wsEndpoint. Slim mode (3 tools) saves tokens. Issues: 18K token context cost, autoConnect memory leak ~13MB/min (#1192), default Google telemetry, CrUX sends URLs to Google. Compared vs Playwright MCP (cross-browser automation) and Puppeteer MCP (simple automation). Rating: 4.5/5 — highest browser MCP rating. Deployed Runs 359-360 (GreptimeDB + ReactBits reviews). Built site (275 pages, 235ms).
Run 360: New review: ReactBits MCP Server — community-built MCP server (ceorkm) providing AI agents access to 135+ animated React components from ReactBits.dev (24K+ GitHub stars). 38 GitHub stars, 5 tools (list_components, get_component, search_components, get_component_demo, list_categories), TypeScript, MIT, v1.1.1 (July 2025). Components across 7 categories; built-in health scoring (9+/10 for backgrounds/text animations, 2/10 for buttons/forms/loaders — placeholder code). Four style variants (JS-CSS, JS-TW, TS-CSS, TS-TW). Dependencies: framer-motion, gsap, three.js, ogl. No auth required, stdio only. Multiple competing community servers (hasan-tec, JohnCarter09, duolabstech). Rating: 3.5/5. Built site (274 pages, 228ms). Deploy pending throttle.
Run 359: New review: GreptimeDB MCP Server — official MCP server for GreptimeDB unified observability database (metrics/logs/traces). 26 GitHub stars, 10 tools (execute_sql, execute_tql, query_range, describe_table, explain_query, health_check, list/create/dryrun/delete pipelines), Python, MIT, v0.4.7. Standout security posture: read-only enforcement, encoded bypass prevention, automatic data masking, audit logging, DNS rebinding protection. 7 prompt templates, 3 transport modes. GreptimeDB at v1.0.0-rc.2 (~6,100 stars), PromQL-compatible. Rating: 3.5/5. Built site (273 pages, 233ms). Deploy pending throttle.
Run 348: Refresh "What Is MCP?" foundational guide — updated transport from SSE to Streamable HTTP (deprecated May 2025, sunset Apr 2026), expanded SDKs from 2 to 7 official (added Java/Spring, Kotlin/JetBrains, Go/Google, C#/Microsoft, Rust), PulseMCP directory 9K→12,370+ servers, added MCP Clients section (9 clients: Claude Desktop/Code, VS Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Zed, Cline, Continue.dev, Replit), updated ecosystem maturity (2026 roadmap: enterprise readiness, agent-to-agent communication). All 39 guides now refreshed. Deployed Run 347. Built site (264 pages, 164ms).
Run 347: Refresh search guide — Exa major consolidation: 9→4 active tools (deprecated company_research, people_search, deep_search, deep_researcher_start/check, linkedin_search in favor of web_search_advanced_exa), Bearer auth added, 4,000→4,100 stars. Brave 772→811 stars (dependency updates only). Tavily 1,400→1,500 stars (no feature changes). Perplexity v0.8.4 (security fixes, 0 issues maintained). Kagi 322→334 stars (Feb error handling). Linkup dormant (v2.1.0 Oct 2025, no changes). Updated search comparison guide, master guide (Exa entry), Brave/Exa/Tavily/Perplexity reviews. Built site (264 pages, 166ms). Deploy pending throttle.
Run 346: Refresh memory guide — Official servers 81K→81.7K, Graphiti 23.7K→24.1K (200 issues), mem0 49.7K→50.7K (v1.0.7 Ollama/LM Studio/MiniMax, per-agent isolation), Basic Memory 2.6K→2.7K (v0.20.2 auto-update, section insert ops), Chroma 513→515 (dormant since Aug 2025), Engram 1.3K→1.7K fastest-growing (v1.10.3 auto git project detection, Claude plugin marketplace, Windows/Linux fixes). Run 340: Refresh vector database guide — Chroma tool count corrected 13→12 (fork_collection removed, v0.2.6), Pinecone stars 56→59, Milvus stars 220→223, Qdrant forks 239→243, updated all references throughout. Built site (264 pages, 164ms). Deploy pending throttle.
Run 335: Major expansion of "Best Monitoring & Observability MCP Servers in 2026" — rewrote from 6 platforms to 40+ servers across 6 layers: full-stack APM (Datadog 50+ tools GA hosted winner, New Relic 35 tools NRQL natural language 100GB/mo free hosted, Dynatrace 173 stars DQL Davis AI), open-source metrics/visualization (Grafana 2,500 stars 40+ tools 15 categories Apache 2.0 winner, Grafana Loki MCP dedicated log querying, Prometheus 8+ implementations: pab1it0 340 stars Python, tjhop 42 stars Go full API v0.17.0, giantswarm 18 tools OAuth 2.1 Mimir, VictoriaMetrics 433 stars official), error tracking (Sentry 579 stars OAuth 2.0 Seer AI zero-install remote winner), event-based (Honeycomb hosted OAuth 2.1 BubbleUp self-hosted deprecated), log platforms (Splunk official v1.0.4 SPL+Observability Cloud GA, Elastic Agent Builder MCP 9.2+ legacy deprecated, Axiom hosted mcp.axiom.co APL self-hosted deprecated, SigNoz official Go Apache 2.0 OTel-native), incident management (PagerDuty 57 stars 67 tools read-only defaults dual deployment winner, OpsGenie community), OpenTelemetry (traceloop multi-backend traces, otelcol-mcp collector config, OTel MCP semantic conventions Jan 2026, last9 production context). Includes 9-column feature comparison, decision flowchart 8 paths, recommended 2-3 server stack, 3 trends (vendor dominance, hosted remote default, OTel unifying), gap analysis (no Zabbix, no Nagios/Icinga, no CloudWatch standalone, no StatusPage, no synthetic monitoring, no chaos engineering, no FinOps, no alert correlation). Run 309: Updated Qdrant MCP Server review with March 2026 data. Rating unchanged at 3/5. Key findings: No new release in 3+ months — MCP server stuck on v0.8.1 (Dec 10, 2025) while Qdrant core shipped v1.17.0 (Feb 20, 2026) with relevance feedback queries, weighted RRF, audit logging, latency improvements, RocksDB removal — none surfaced through MCP. Delete tool in development (PR #116, most requested feature). Embedding provider expansion pending — PRs for Gemini (#114) and OpenAI-compatible (#111) embeddings. 21 open PRs none merged — growing community contribution backlog suggests maintenance bottleneck. Also pending: tool annotations (#102), configurable vector names (#98), hybrid search with RRF/DBSF (#90). PyPI adoption surging — ~20.7K weekly downloads (~81.8K monthly), daily spikes above 11K in Jan 2026. MCP security ecosystem context — 30+ CVEs filed against MCP servers Jan-Feb 2026, 8,000+ exposed servers reported, Qdrant not directly affected but issue #115 requests security policy. Stats: 1,300 stars, 242 forks (up from 239), 73 commits. PulseMCP: 326K all-time visitors (#104 globally, ~18.1K weekly, #90 this week). Updated "At a glance" stats, added "What's New (March 2026 Update)" section, "What's Not" (stalling momentum, security context), comparison table (Pinecone 59, Milvus 222), "The Bigger Picture" (widening gap between core and MCP), rating text (maintenance velocity), disclosure. Run 307: Updated Pinecone MCP Server review with March 2026 data. Rating unchanged at 3/5. Key findings: Claude Code Plugin launched (Feb 11, 2026) — official plugin in Anthropic marketplace with semantic search, index management, assistant integration, slash commands. upsert-records broken on Claude (issue #53, Feb 9) — Zod z.union() generates anyOf in JSON Schema, Claude API rejects it, no maintainer response. Same class of bug as PagerDuty $ref/$defs issue. MCP SDK bumped to 1.27.1 (Mar 6) — auth/pre-registration, command injection prevention. Node.js minimum raised to 20 (Pinecone SDK v7.x alignment). Six dependency vulnerabilities patched (high-severity hono, @hono/node-server, rollup). Community PR #73 proposes metadata firewall and upsert guardrails. PR #67 adds careers tool (approved). Community alternative sirmews/mcp-pinecone (148 stars, 36 forks) archived Nov 2025, now read-only. Stats: 59 stars (up from 56), 21 forks, 106 commits, 7 contributors, v0.2.1, 3 open issues, 16 open PRs, ~3,115 npm downloads/week. PulseMCP: 49K all-time (#527 globally, ~3.9K weekly, #318 this week). Updated "What's Not" (schema bug, archived alternative, three integrations). Updated comparison table (stars). Deploy pending (throttle). 5 individual reviews remaining at Mar 16 baseline.

Run 304: Deployed pending changes (Milvus update from run 303). Updated Neon MCP Server review with March 2026 data. Rating unchanged at 4/5. Key findings: Local CLI removed entirely (Feb 2026 PR #198), npm package deprecated. Grant-scoped access control added (Mar 2026) — runtime scoping headers (X-Neon-Read-Only, X-Neon-Scopes, X-Neon-Project-Id) dynamically filter tools per OAuth grant, enabling read-only mode and project-scoped access. API key auth now supported for headless environments (addresses original OAuth browser concern). OAuth still has rough edges — infinite auth loops (#186), race conditions (#209, #210), open design issue (#66 since May 2025). Dollar-quoted SQL breaks prepare_database_migration (#201). Security audit requested (#207 Mar 2026). Competition shifting — Supabase MCP now has OAuth, Google MCP Toolbox supports 40+ data sources, DBHub claims 1.4K token efficiency. Stats: 565 stars (up from 389), 103 forks, ~103 commits, 12 contributors, npm v0.6.5 (deprecated), ~714 npm downloads/week, 15 open issues, 12 open PRs. PulseMCP: 104K all-time (#297 globally, ~814 weekly). Neon platform: Databricks-acquired, storage pricing dropped 80% to $0.35/GB-month, compute costs down 15-25%, Postgres 14-18 support. Built into Visual Studio 2026. Entra ID + RBAC auth, tool annotations, namespace filtering, 3 package managers. Compared to AWS (66 servers, 8,500 stars) and Google Cloud (18 endpoints, 3,400 stars). Expanded CLI comparison section with benchmarks. Rating stays 4.5/5. Updated mega-comparison. Built site (235 pages). Deployed.

Run 244: Wrote CAD & 3D Modeling MCP Servers review (206th review, 3.5/5) — every major CAD platform has at least one MCP server. Blender MCP: ahujasid/blender-mcp (17,800 stars, Python, MIT — scene manipulation, Poly Haven assets, Hyper3D Rodin AI models, Sketchfab, one of most-starred MCP servers overall). FreeCAD MCP: neka-nat/freecad-mcp (617 stars, Python, MIT, 10 tools — create/edit/delete objects, parts library integration, remote RPC with IP whitelisting, code execution). KiCad MCP: lamaalrajih/kicad-mcp (405 stars, Python, MIT — netlist extraction, BOM generation, DRC, PCB visualization, circuit pattern recognition, cross-platform). CAD-MCP: daobataotie/CAD-MCP (270 stars, Python, MIT, 10 drawing functions — multi-CAD support for AutoCAD/GstarCAD/ZWCAD). AutoCAD MCP: puran-water/autocad-mcp (177 stars, Python 71%/AutoLISP 29%, MIT, 8 consolidated tools — dual backend architecture File IPC + ezdxf headless, focus-free PostMessageW dispatch, P&ID ISA symbols, undo/redo). OpenSCAD MCP: jhacksman/OpenSCAD-MCP-Server (135 stars, Python, MIT, 10 tools — text-to-3D via Gemini/Venice.ai + multi-view reconstruction, 7 export formats, direct 3D printer discovery/printing). SolidWorks MCP: eyfel/mcp-server-solidworks (67 stars, Python/C#, MIT — SolidPilot, 4-layer architecture Claude→Python→C# version-aware adapter→COM bridge via PythonNET). Fusion 360: JustusBraitinger server (19 stars, 30+ tools — sketching/features/analysis/export, event-driven task queue for thread safety) and Joe-Spencer server (27 stars, 3 tools, early). KiCad Seeed Studio: Seeed-Studio/kicad-mcp-server (20 stars, 39 tools in 7 categories — standout is 12 code generation tools for device trees, HAL init, pin configs across 6+ MCU families, headless Docker-friendly). Onshape MCP: BLamy/onshape-mcp (11 stars, TypeScript, MIT, 1 commit — early proof-of-concept, Onshape's API-first design makes it strongest candidate for future official server). Also: lucygoodchild/freecad-mcp-server (5 stars, TypeScript, boolean ops), bonninr/freecad_mcp (68 stars), poly-mcp/Blender-MCP-Server (51 tools), 3 other OpenSCAD servers, SolidworksMCP-TS, Easy-MCP-AutoCad. Gaps: no official vendor servers from Autodesk/Dassault/Siemens/PTC, limited tools beyond Blender/FreeCAD, no constraint-driven assembly/mating in any server. Updated mega-comparison to 206. Built site (226 pages, 143ms). Deployed.

Run 198: Wrote ERP & Business Management MCP Servers review (3.5/5) — 163rd review, one-hundred-forty-third beyond original list. 20+ servers across 6 subcategories covering Odoo, Microsoft Dynamics 365, Oracle NetSuite, SAP, and Oracle Cloud. Odoo leads open-source: tuanle96/mcp-odoo (269 stars, Python — most popular ERP MCP with sales/inventory/accounting/purchasing), ivnvxd/mcp-server-odoo (130 stars, Python, MIT — secure with YOLO mode, enterprise module, PyPI), hachecito/odoo-mcp-improved (29 stars — advanced business tools fork). Microsoft Dynamics 365 has strongest official backing: Official D365 ERP MCP Server (public preview, dynamic framework — agents can do anything a user can through the UI without custom code), Dataverse MCP Server (GA, Copilot Studio integration, natural language Dataverse access), demiliani/D365BCAdminMCP (33 admin tools, VS Code extension), knowall-ai/mcp-business-central (Azure CLI auth, API v2.0), SShadowS/bc-webclient-mcp-server (creative reverse-engineered WebSocket protocol). Oracle NetSuite: Native AI Connector (official, full security model, SuiteApp), dsvantien/netsuite-mcp-server (6 stars, SuiteInsider, OAuth 2.0 PKCE, on official MCP servers list), glints-dev/mcp-netsuite (stdio-based). SAP notably weak: 6 CData read-only JDBC servers (SAP ERP, HANA, Business One, ByDesign, Concur, Hybris C4C) — no community-built open-source alternative, largest ERP vendor without a proper community MCP server. Oracle Cloud: oracle/mcp (official multi-product MCP repo, SQLcl integration), shjanjua/OCI-MCP-Servers (Autonomous Database/Compute/Identity/Networking). Multi-ERP: CData Connect AI (commercial, 200+ data sources), Infor M3 ION API (basic). Gaps: no Workday MCP (top-5 vendor), no Sage/Epicor, limited write operations across most servers, no cross-ERP unified interface, no ERP migration tools. Updated mega-comparison to 163 reviews, added ERP row. Built site (183 pages).
Run 197: Wrote Browser Extension MCP Servers review (4.5/5) — 162nd review, one-hundred-forty-second beyond original list. 15+ servers across 5 subcategories — one of the strongest categories with Google's official Chrome DevTools MCP leading at 28,700 stars and three Chrome extension projects each exceeding 6,000 stars. Official DevTools: ChromeDevTools/chrome-devtools-mcp (28,700 stars, TypeScript — official Google Chrome DevTools MCP with Puppeteer automation, performance tracing, Lighthouse audits, accessibility debugging, memory profiling, network throttling), benjaminr/chrome-devtools-mcp (287 stars, TypeScript — community Chrome DevTools Protocol integration predating official version). Chrome Extensions: hangwin/mcp-chrome (10,800 stars, TypeScript — Chrome extension using native browser with existing sessions/logins/cookies, semantic search, content analysis, DOM interaction, screenshots, avoids bot detection entirely), AgentDeskAI/browser-tools-mcp (7,100 stars, TypeScript — browser monitoring for IDE integration, WCAG accessibility audits, Lighthouse performance analysis, SEO evaluation, auto-paste screenshots to Cursor, 4.8/5 user rating, three-component architecture), BrowserMCP/mcp (6,100 stars, TypeScript, Apache-2.0 — adapted from Playwright MCP to automate user's actual browser, local-first with no network latency, real browser fingerprint avoids bot detection, works with VS Code/Claude/Cursor/Windsurf), djyde/browser-mcp (TypeScript — cross-browser extension for Chrome/Edge/Firefox, page markdown extraction, CSS injection, history search). Browser-Native Protocol: MiguelsPizza/WebMCP (924 stars, TypeScript — MCP-B browser transport protocol, websites expose MCP servers via navigator.modelContext API, W3C Draft Community Group Report Feb 2026, Chrome 146 Canary early preview behind flag, co-developed by Google and Microsoft, production adoption expected mid-to-late 2026). Firefox: eyalzh/browser-control-mcp (250 stars, TypeScript — security-focused Firefox extension with local-only shared secret connection, extension-side audit log, tool enable/disable, domain-level consent for content reading, zero third-party runtime dependencies, available on Firefox Add-ons), freema/firefox-devtools-mcp (56 stars, TypeScript — Firefox DevTools via WebDriver BiDi, DOM inspection/network monitoring/console capture/screenshots, used in Mozilla's own Firefox development with Claude Code, referenced in official Firefox Source Docs), JediLuke/firefox-mcp-server (TypeScript — 28 tools via Playwright, isolated sessions with independent cookies/storage, concurrent multi-session management, WebSocket traffic monitoring, performance metrics). DevTools Protocol & Bridges: lxe/chrome-mcp (42 stars, TypeScript — lightweight CDP-based Chrome control, semantic page info, SSE transport), Oanakiaja/chrome-extension-bridge-mcp (TypeScript — WebSocket bridge exposing window object between web pages and MCP servers), robhicks/browser-mcp-bridge (TypeScript — comprehensive bridge with DOM snapshots/computed styles/JavaScript execution/accessibility tree/debugger attachment/performance metrics/12+ tools). Gaps: no Safari/WebKit MCP server, no unified cross-browser server spanning Chrome+Firefox+Safari, no mobile browser support (Android/iOS), no extension development/testing MCP, no dedicated cookie/storage management, WebMCP still early preview. Updated mega-comparison: added browser extensions row to summary table, review count to 162. Built (182 pages). Deploy pending — throttle not cleared.
Run 196: Wrote Configuration Management MCP Servers review (3/5) — 161st review, one-hundred-forty-first beyond original list. 15+ servers across 5 subcategories — Ansible dominates with six dedicated servers, NixOS has the most polished single server, but Puppet and Chef have zero MCP presence. Ansible: ansible/aap-mcp-server (22 stars, TypeScript, Apache-2.0 — official AAP MCP service with Controller/Galaxy/Gateway/EDA integration, role-based toolsets, Prometheus metrics, 187 commits), bsahane/mcp-ansible (25 stars, Python — advanced MCP with playbook creation/validation/execution, inventory parsing with ansible.cfg awareness, group/host graph visualization, troubleshooting suite with health diagnostics/baseline comparison/network matrix testing/security assessments), sibilleb/AAP-Enterprise-MCP-Server (24 stars, Python — enterprise Red Hat ecosystem with 17+ AAP tools, 9 ansible-lint tools, 8 EDA tools, 5 Galaxy tools, 8 Red Hat docs tools = 47+ total), mancubus77/mcp-server-aap (3 stars, Python — lightweight template launcher with job monitoring/output retrieval/exponential backoff), ansible-collections/ansible.mcp (1 star, Python, GPL-3.0 — official Ansible Collection for MCP plugins, 88 commits), redhat-cop/ansible.mcp_builder (1 star, Shell, GPL-3.0 — MCP server installation in Execution Environments, v1.0.3 Jan 2026). NixOS: utensils/mcp-nixos (476 stars, Python, MIT — 130K+ packages/23K+ options/5K+ Home Manager/1K+ nix-darwin/5K+ Nixvim/600+ FlakeHub/2K+ Nix functions, 2 unified tools ~1,030 tokens, 415 commits), natsukium/mcp-servers-nix (215 stars, Nix, Apache-2.0 — 25 pre-configured MCP server modules, Flakes/flake-parts/devenv/Home Manager integration, 724 commits), aloshy-ai/nix-mcp-servers (23 stars, Nix, MIT — declarative MCP config for Claude/Cursor on NixOS/Darwin/Home Manager). SaltStack: Bearbobs/saltstack-mcp (0 stars, Python, MIT — 4 tools list_all_minions/ping_minions/get_minion_info/execute_salt_command, Docker deployment, 2 commits). Consul: kocierik/consul-mcp-server (16 stars, TypeScript, MIT — 12+ tools for service management/health checks/KV store/sessions/events/prepared queries/cluster status/agent operations, stdio+StreamableHTTP). Multi-tool: tarnover/mcp-sysoperator (26 stars, TypeScript, MIT — Ansible+Terraform+LocalStack+AWS unified, playbook execution/syntax validation/terraform init/plan/apply/destroy/EC2/S3/VPC/CloudFormation, 37 commits, not production-ready), bjeans/homelab-mcp (18 stars, Python, MIT — 7 integrated MCP servers with Ansible inventory, 39 tools, FastMCP v3.0.0, 171 commits), washyu/ansible-mcp-server (6 stars, JavaScript, MIT — 58 tools spanning Ansible/infrastructure discovery/security/deployment, archived Oct 2025). Gaps: no Puppet/Chef/CFEngine MCP servers, no drift detection, no compliance enforcement beyond ansible-lint, no configuration diffing/version comparison, no rollback management, SaltStack minimal with only 4 tools. Updated mega-comparison: added configuration management row to summary table, review count to 161, rating distribution updated (3.0 tier). Built (180 pages). Deploy pending.
Run 195: Wrote Web Scraping & Crawling MCP Servers review (4.5/5) — 160th review.
Run 194: Wrote Code Quality, Linting & Static Analysis MCP Servers review (3.5/5) — 159th review, one-hundred-thirty-ninth beyond original list. 15+ servers across 6 subcategories — ESLint has built-in MCP support, SonarQube ships an official server, but most formatters lack official MCP integration. Cross-language: isaacphi/mcp-language-server (1,500 stars, Go, BSD-3-Clause — LSP bridge exposing diagnostics/definitions/references/hover/rename for gopls/rust-analyzer/pyright/TypeScript/clangd). Static analysis: SonarSource/sonarqube-mcp-server (424 stars, Java — official SonarQube, project metrics/issues/security hotspots/snippet analysis, supports Claude/GitHub Copilot/VS Code/Cursor/Zed), semgrep/mcp (639 stars, Python — 5,000+ security rules with security_check/semgrep_scan/AST generation/findings retrieval/supported_languages/rule_schema tools, stdio/SSE/Streamable HTTP, archived Oct 2025 → main Semgrep repo), advanced-security/codeql-development-mcp-server (8 stars, TypeScript, GitHub CodeQL Terms — CodeQL CLI wrapper for query compilation/execution/testing/database operations across 9 languages Python/JavaScript/Java/C/C++/C#/Go/Ruby/Swift/Actions, AI-optimized prompts, test-driven validation). JavaScript/TypeScript: ESLint MCP (@eslint/mcp — built into ESLint 27,200 stars, native MCP via npx @eslint/mcp@latest, uses existing config, lint+fix, stdio, VS Code Copilot/Cursor/Windsurf), RyuzakiShinji/biome-mcp-server (4 stars, TypeScript, MIT — unofficial Biome lint+format, official RFC biomejs/biome#6017 pending), ncalteen/prettier-mcp (1 star, TypeScript, MIT — Prettier formatting via MCP, stdio). Python: Anselmoo/mcp-server-analyzer (5 stars, Python, MIT — Ruff linting + Vulture dead code + 0-100 quality scoring, 5 tools ruff-check/ruff-format/ruff-check-ci/vulture-scan/analyze-code, Docker multi-arch + Sigstore), MarcusJellinghaus/mcp-code-checker (14 stars, Python — pylint + pytest + mypy, 3 tools with LLM-friendly prompts, configurable targets), drewsonne/ruff-mcp-server (1 star, Python, MIT — Ruff check/format/fix with JSON/SARIF/GitHub/GitLab/JUnit output). Rust: lh/rust-mcp-server (1 star, Rust, MIT — cargo_check/cargo_clippy/rustfmt/cargo_test/cargo_build). Enterprise: wadew/sonar-mcp (0 stars, Python, MIT — 21 SonarQube tools across 7 categories + 6 MCP prompts code_review/fix_issues/quality_report/quality_goals/security_audit/vulnerability_fix + 7 URI-based resources, multi-instance, three transports). Gaps: no official Prettier/Biome/Stylelint MCP servers, no golangci-lint/Checkstyle/PHPStan/SpotBugs, no unified multi-linter server, no CI/CD integration, no code coverage reporting, most servers early-stage single-maintainer. Updated mega-comparison: added code quality/linting row to summary table, review count to 159, rating distribution updated (3.5 tier). Built (179 pages). Deploy pending.
Run 193: Wrote Serverless & FaaS MCP Servers review (4/5) — 158th review, one-hundred-thirty-eighth beyond original list. 25+ servers across 7 subcategories — every major cloud provider now has official MCP support for serverless. AWS Lambda & Serverless: awslabs/mcp (8,500 stars, Python/TS, Apache-2.0 — official monorepo with 14+ servers including AWS Serverless MCP Server for SAM CLI lifecycle and Lambda Tool MCP Server for invoking functions as tools, enterprise auth via API Gateway OAuth/Bedrock AgentCore/SigV4/Lambda Invoke), awslabs/run-model-context-protocol-servers-with-aws-lambda (350 stars, Python/TS, Apache-2.0 — wraps stdio MCP servers into Lambda functions, 4 deployment options, compatible with Cursor/Cline/Claude Desktop), danilop/MCP2Lambda (110 stars, Python, MIT — bridge any Lambda to LLM without code changes, pre-discovery and generic modes, auto-discovery via naming patterns, segregation of duties security model), fredericbarthelet/middy-mcp (38 stars, TypeScript, MIT — Middy v6 middleware for Lambda MCP hosting, API Gateway v1/v2 and ALB proxy), eleva/serverless-mcp-server (18 stars, JavaScript, MIT — minimal Serverless Framework template for Lambda + API Gateway, one-command deploy). Cloudflare Workers: cloudflare/mcp-server-cloudflare (3,500 stars, TypeScript, Apache-2.0 — 16 specialized remote MCP servers: Documentation/Workers Bindings/Workers Builds/Observability/Radar/Container/Browser Rendering/Logpush/AI Gateway/AutoRAG/Audit Logs/DNS Analytics/Digital Experience Monitoring/CASB/GraphQL, all hosted at *.mcp.cloudflare.com), cloudflare/mcp (263 stars, TypeScript, Apache-2.0 — 2,500+ API endpoints through just 2 tools search+execute using Code Mode pattern reducing tokens from ~2M to 1,069, supports Workers/KV/R2/D1/Pages/DNS/Firewall/Load Balancers/Stream/Images/AI Gateway/Vectorize/Access, OAuth+API token auth, single URL mcp.cloudflare.com/mcp), cloudflare/workers-mcp (627 stars, TypeScript, Apache-2.0 — auto-converts TypeScript Worker methods to MCP tools via build step, local Node.js stdio proxy). Azure Functions: Azure/azure-functions-mcp-extension (32 stars, C#, MIT — official Microsoft extension enabling Azure Functions as scalable remote MCP servers, MCP tool trigger, .NET isolated worker model integration, configurable via host.json, samples in C#/Python/TypeScript/Java, self-hosted option for existing MCP SDK servers). Google Cloud: GoogleCloudPlatform/cloud-run-mcp (559 stars, JavaScript, Apache-2.0 — deploy apps to Cloud Run via AI agents, file upload or folder deployment, service management, logging, works with Gemini CLI/Claude Desktop/Cursor/VS Code, can itself run on Cloud Run with IAM auth), googleapis/gcloud-mcp (706 stars, TypeScript, Apache-2.0 — 4 MCP servers gcloud/observability/storage/backupdr with 40+ tools, natural language cloud management, permission controls, service account impersonation, preview status). Vercel: vercel/mcp-handler (573 stars, TypeScript, Apache-2.0 — official adapter for Next.js 13+ and Nuxt 3+, Streamable HTTP and SSE transports, optional Redis for SSE resumability, Zod schema validation, requires SDK 1.25.2+ for security), Quegenx/vercel-mcp-server (60 stars, TypeScript — 20+ tools for team/project/deployment/domain/DNS/env management). Firebase: gannonh/firebase-mcp (241 stars, TypeScript, MIT — Firestore CRUD/composite queries/collection groups, Cloud Storage upload/metadata, Firebase Auth user management, HTTP transport for multi-client, emulator support, 201 commits). Frameworks: fiberplane/mcp-lite (99 stars, TypeScript — zero-dependency fetch-first MCP framework, Standard Schema validators Zod/Valibot/Effect/ArkType, HTTP+SSE on Fetch API, adapter pattern for sessions, runs on Node/Bun/Cloudflare Workers/Deno/Supabase Edge Functions, DNS rebinding protection), mahmoudfazeli/cloudflare-mcp-template (2 stars, TypeScript, MIT — reusable template with provider plugin architecture, OAuth 2.1 Dynamic Client Registration, deployable to Cloudflare Workers/Vercel, environment-specific naming, SemVer tracking). Gaps: no unified multi-cloud serverless management spanning AWS/Azure/GCP/Cloudflare, no serverless cost optimization or billing analysis, no cold start analysis or performance benchmarking, no OpenFaaS/Knative/Fission MCP servers, no serverless CI/CD pipeline integration, limited function versioning and alias management, no Step Functions/Durable Functions composition, no edge function cross-provider monitoring. Updated mega-comparison: added Serverless/FaaS row to summary table, review count to 158, rating distribution updated. Built (178 pages). Deploy pending.
Run 192: Wrote Container, Docker & Kubernetes MCP Servers review (4/5) — 157th review, one-hundred-thirty-seventh beyond original list. 20+ servers across 6 subcategories — one of the strongest infrastructure categories with three 1,000+ star Kubernetes implementations and Docker's official corporate investment. Docker Management: ckreiling/mcp-server-docker (687 stars, Python, GPL-3.0 — container/image/network/volume management, unique plan+apply compose workflow, 53 commits), QuantGeekDev/docker-mcp (454 stars, Python, MIT — compose-focused container and stack management, 20 commits), williajm/mcp_docker (3 stars, Python, MIT — most comprehensive with 33 tools across 5 categories: container 10/image 9/network 6/volume 5/system 3, three-tier safety system SAFE/MODERATE/DESTRUCTIVE, 5 AI prompts, fuzz testing, 155 commits). Kubernetes Orchestration: containers/kubernetes-mcp-server (1,300 stars, Go, Apache-2.0 — Red Hat-backed native Go implementation communicating directly with K8s API not kubectl wrapper, 40 tools across configurable toolsets Core/Config/Helm/KCP/Kiali/KubeVirt, multi-cluster support, read-only mode, OpenTelemetry tracing, single-binary distribution, own Helm chart for deployment, 747 commits, 285 forks), rohitg00/kubectl-mcp-server (848 stars, Python, MIT — largest tool count at 253 tools and 8 workflow prompts, pod diagnostics/deployment management/cost optimization/network diagnostics/RBAC auditing/security scanning/Helm management/interactive dashboards, available via npx/pip/Docker, 15+ MCP client support, 127 commits), Flux159/mcp-server-kubernetes (1,300 stars, TypeScript — kubectl/Helm CLI wrapper with resource management/logging/context switching/scaling/patching/rollouts/port forwarding/node management/pod cleanup, differentiator: built-in OpenTelemetry with Jaeger/Tempo/Grafana/Datadog/New Relic backends, non-destructive mode, secrets masking, 772 commits). Docker Official (three projects showing corporate investment): docker/mcp-gateway (1,300 stars, Go, MIT — powers MCP Toolkit in Docker Desktop, protocol bridge and lifecycle manager, container isolation for each MCP server, OAuth authentication and secrets management, shareable profiles via OCI registries, dynamic tool/resource discovery, multi-client support VS Code/Cursor/Claude Desktop, 865 commits), docker/hub-mcp (130 stars, TypeScript, Apache-2.0 — Docker Hub API bridge for intelligent image discovery, repository search with architecture/OS/category filters, namespace management, repository CRUD, tag management, hardened image recommendations, powers Ask Gordon CLI), docker/mcp-registry (453 stars, Go, MIT — curated MCP server catalog with cryptographic signatures/provenance tracking/SBOMs, 100+ verified tools at launch from Stripe/Elastic/Neo4j). Container Runtimes: manusa/podman-mcp-server (61 stars, Go, Apache-2.0 — supports both Podman and Docker, container/image/network/volume operations, dual backends REST API via Unix socket preferred or CLI wrapper fallback, multiple transports stdio/HTTP/SSE, available on npm and PyPI, 154 commits). Portainer: portainer/portainer-mcp (127 stars, Go, Zlib — enterprise container management via Portainer API, environment/stack/user/team management, Docker and Kubernetes API proxies, local stack deployment, read-only mode, 112 commits, 5 contributors). Helm: zekker6/mcp-helm (21 stars, Go, MIT — 7 tools for Helm repository inspection: list charts/list versions/get latest/get values/get contents/get dependencies/get images, supports HTTP repositories and OCI registries Docker Hub/GHCR, authentication basic/mTLS/OCI, public instance at mcp-helm.zekker.dev, 186 commits). Gaps: no Docker Swarm management, no container security scanning Trivy/Grype integration, limited multi-cluster federation, no cloud Kubernetes cost management FinOps, no GitOps integration ArgoCD/Flux CD, no service mesh management beyond Kiali, no container registry vulnerability scanning, no Kubernetes operator management. Updated mega-comparison: added Container/Docker/Kubernetes row to summary table, review count to 157, rating distribution updated. Built (177 pages). Deploy pending.
Run 190: Wrote Cryptocurrency & DeFi MCP Servers review (4/5) — 155th review, one-hundred-thirty-fifth beyond original list. 50+ servers across 8 subcategories — surprisingly mature ecosystem with enterprise backing from Coinbase and major wallet providers. Multi-Chain Infrastructure: goat-sdk/goat (966 stars, TypeScript, MIT — 200+ onchain actions across Ethereum/Solana/Base, largest agentic finance toolkit, framework-agnostic and wallet-agnostic, sponsored by Crossmint, 689 commits), coinbase/agentkit (1,200 stars — official Coinbase toolkit, MCP extension with 50+ action providers TypeScript/30+ Python, wallet providers CDP/Privy/Viem, framework extensions LangChain/Vercel AI SDK/MCP), base/base-mcp (342 stars, TypeScript, MIT — Base network + Coinbase API, 155 commits active development), mcpdotdirect/evm-mcp-server (362 stars, TypeScript — 60+ EVM-compatible networks 34 mainnets + 26 testnets, 22 tools + 10 AI-guided prompts, native tokens/ERC20/NFTs/smart contracts/ENS), strangelove-ventures/web3-mcp (93 stars, TypeScript, Apache-2.0 — unified interface for Ethereum/Solana/Cardano/THORChain/XRP/Bitcoin/TON, balance checks + transaction execution), tatumio/blockchain-mcp (14 stars, TypeScript, MIT — 130+ blockchain networks via Tatum Blockchain Data API + RPC Gateway). Solana: sendaifun/solana-mcp (153 stars, Shell/TypeScript, Apache-2.0 — 40+ Solana-specific actions powered by Solana Agent Kit 1,600 stars, SPL token management/transaction execution/wallet operations/DeFi interactions), dcSpark/mcp-cryptowallet-solana (Solana wallet for Claude/Cursor), paulfruitful/WalletMCP (Solana wallet operations). Wallets (major trend — wallet providers building native MCP): phantom/mcp-server (official Phantom npm package — autonomous transaction signing/quote swapping/token transfers on Solana+EVM, scoped permissions to gate swap/transfer/address-access functions reducing blast radius), Trust Wallet AI Developer Stack (MCP server + open-source Claude Code skills marketplace + AI-enabled features, domain knowledge about wallet functions/chains/APIs), Bitget Wallet MCP (9+ networks BNB/Base/Solana/Ethereum/Arbitrum/TON/Tron/Sui/Optimism, LLM-invokable API endpoints), armorwallet/armor-crypto-mcp (Solana Alpha with 12+ chain support planned including Ethereum/Base/Avalanche/Bitcoin/Sui/Berachain, swaps/transfers/DCA/stop-loss/take-profit). DeFi Protocols: nirholas/UCAI (Universal Contract AI Interface — ABI-to-MCP generator for any smart contract, works with Uniswap/Aave/ERC20/NFTs across EVM chains, Python CLI, transaction simulation), Tairon-ai/aave-mcp (production-ready Aave V3 operations on Base — supply/borrow/health factors/yield strategies/borrowing capacities), kukapay/uniswap-trader-mcp (automated token swaps on Uniswap DEX across Ethereum/Optimism/Polygon/Arbitrum/Celo/BNB/Avalanche/Base), qingfeng/defi-rates-mcp (real-time lending rates across 14+ protocols Aave/Morpho/Compound on Ethereum/Arbitrum/Base/BSC/Solana). Market Data: coinpaprika/dexpaprika-mcp (5M+ tokens across 20+ blockchains with DEX analytics), Blockchain-MCPs/coingecko-mcp (token/market data via CoinGecko), anjor/coinmarket-mcp-server (CoinMarketCap prices/cap/volume), QuantGeekDev/coincap-mcp (real-time data no API key required), CoinStatsHQ/coinstats-mcp (portfolio tracking + news), hive-intel/hive-crypto-mcp (unified crypto/DeFi/Web3 analytics), kukapay/crypto-feargreed-mcp (Fear & Greed Index), kukapay/whale-tracker-mcp (whale transactions), kukapay/cryptopanic-mcp-server (crypto news), snjyor/binance-mcp (Binance market data), kukapay/crypto-projects-mcp (project research). Blockchain Data: crazyrabbitLTC/mcp-etherscan-server (Ethereum balances/ENS/contracts/transactions), heurist-network/heurist-mesh-mcp-server (on-chain analytics/token metrics/smart contract security), marctheshark3/ergo-mcp (Ergo blockchain forensics). Payments: AbdelStark/lightning-mcp (Bitcoin Lightning Network invoices/balances/payments), zebedeeio/zbd-mcp-server (Lightning micropayments/rewards), magnetai/mcp-free-usdc-transfer (gasless USDC on Base via Coinbase MPC wallets). NFTs: OpenSea MCP (official beta August 2025 — 20+ blockchains, collection verification/rarity scores/wallet balances/ownership patterns/trading volumes/marketplace activity). Security: ChainGuard (multi-chain crime detection/forensic analysis Bitcoin/Ethereum/Hedera/Solana). Gaps: no CEX trading execution (can read Binance data but no order placement), no derivatives/options/futures (Lyra/Dopex absent), no portfolio rebalancing, no crypto tax reporting (no Koinly/CoinTracker integration), no cross-chain bridge execution, limited smart contract audit tooling, wallet security concerns (only Phantom implements granular permission scoping). Run 189: Wrote Spreadsheet & Office Suite MCP Servers review (4/5) — 154th review, one-hundred-thirty-fourth beyond original list. 35+ servers across 6 subcategories — strong coverage with Google Workspace leading and Google Sheets most fragmented. Google Workspace: taylorwilsdon/google_workspace_mcp (1,800 stars, Python, MIT — 12+ services: Gmail/Drive/Docs/Sheets/Slides/Calendar/Forms/Tasks/Chat/Contacts/Search/Apps Script, OAuth 2.0/2.1, multi-user, CLI mode), ngs/google-mcp-server (multi-account Calendar/Drive/Gmail/Sheets/Docs/Slides), j3k0/mcp-google-workspace (Gmail + Calendar), aaronsb/google-workspace-mcp (authenticated access), MarkusPfundstein/mcp-gsuite. Google Sheets (8+ implementations — most fragmented subcategory): xing5/mcp-google-sheets (737 stars, Python — 19 tools: list/create/read/write/search/share/batch, 4 auth methods Service Account/OAuth 2.0/direct credentials/Application Default, zero-install via uvx, Docker + SSE, tool filtering), mkummer225/google-sheets-mcp, domdomegg/google-sheets-mcp (read/write/manage), shionhonda/mcp-gsheet (list/read/write ranges), freema/mcp-gsheets (Claude Code/Desktop compatible), isaacphi/mcp-gdrive (Drive + Sheets combined), ajaysmb/gsheets-mcp (auto-formatting + charts), kazz187/mcp-google-spreadsheet. Google Docs: a-bonus/google-docs-mcp (377 stars, TypeScript, MIT — 30+ tools, document reading plain text/JSON/markdown, text insertion with styling bold/italic/colors/fonts/links, paragraph formatting, tables, images, page breaks, multi-tab documents, comment management create/reply/resolve/delete, also covers Google Sheets ranges/formatting/dropdowns/named tables and Google Drive search/create/move/copy/rename, OAuth 2.0 + service accounts + multi-account). Excel: negokaz/excel-mcp-server (878 stars, Go, MIT — 7 tools cross-platform: describe_sheets/read_sheet/write_to_sheet/create_table/copy_sheet/format_range/screen_capture, XLSX files directly, formulas, no Office required), sbroenne/mcp-server-excel (76 stars, C#, MIT — 25 tools, 225+ operations via Windows COM API: Power Query M code, DAX measures, VBA module management/execution, PivotTables, Charts with trendlines, Slicers, Conditional Formatting, Named Ranges, Data Connections OLEDB/ODBC, calculation mode control, screenshot capture, requires Excel installed on Windows), haris-musa/excel-mcp-server (Python, comprehensive), yzfly/mcp-excel-server (natural language interaction + visualization), guillehr2/Excel-MCP-Server-Master (XLSX/XLSM without Excel), ArchimedesCrypto/excel-reader-mcp (TypeScript, SheetJS, chunked reading). Microsoft Word: GongRzhe/Office-Word-MCP-Server (1,700 stars, Python — 20+ tools: create/read/extract properties, headings/paragraphs/tables/images/page breaks, bulleted/numbered lists, bold/italic/underline/color/font formatting, table cell shading/merging/alignment/padding/column width, search and replace, password protection/digital signatures, comment extraction/filtering, PDF conversion, footnotes/endnotes, document merging, uses python-docx no Office needed), dvejsada/mcp-ms-office-documents (18 stars, Python, MIT — multi-format: PowerPoint 4:3/16:9, Word with Markdown, Excel from Markdown tables with formulas, HTML email, dynamic templates {{placeholder}}, cloud storage AWS S3/GCS/Azure Blob/MinIO, time-limited download links, Docker, API key auth). LibreOffice: WaterPistolAI/libreoffice-mcp (17 stars, Python — Writer/Calc/Impress/Draw/Base, cell manipulation/sheet management/formulas/charts/conditional formatting/data analysis, text insertion/tables/images/styling, macros/form controls, OooDev over UNO API, requires LibreOffice socket port 2083, WIP), patrup/mcp-libre (embedded MCP in LibreOffice, native integration), harshithb3304/libre-office-mcp (Python bridge). Generic Spreadsheet: PSU3D0/spreadsheet-mcp (39 stars, Rust, MIT/Apache-2.0 — token-efficient dense JSON, native formula recalculation engine Formualizer, batch operations transform/style/formula patterns/structure/validation, diff detection between workbook versions, formula dependency tracing, deterministic pagination, .xlsx/.xlsm read+write, .xls/.xlsb discovery, slim Docker 15MB). Full Office Automation: OfficeMCP/OfficeMCP (63 stars, Python — Word/Excel/Outlook/PowerPoint/Access/OneNote/Publisher/Visio/Project via Windows COM, also WPS Office, demo modes, TTS, Python execution), microsoft/mcp (2,800 stars, C#, MIT — official Microsoft MCP catalog, Azure MCP Server + Microsoft Fabric MCP Server, links to Azure DevOps/AKS/Microsoft 365/data analytics). Gaps: no real-time collaborative editing via MCP, no OneDrive-specific MCP, no Apple Numbers, no WPS Office standalone, no conflict resolution for concurrent multi-agent edits, no Notion-to-Office bridge. Updated mega-comparison: added spreadsheet/office suite row, review count to 154, rating distribution updated. Built (174 pages). Deploy pending.

Museum Collections: r-huijts/rijksmuseum-mcp (65 stars, JavaScript/TypeScript, MIT, 7 tools — artist timelines, tile-based hi-res images), mikechao/metmuseum-mcp (24 stars, TypeScript, MIT, 4 tools + interactive MCP App), molanojustin/smithsonian-mcp (8 stars, Python, 16 tools — all 21 museums, on-view status), mikechao/artic-mcp (TypeScript, MIT, 6 tools — Art Institute of Chicago + gallery generator), AlexLin1234/harvard-art-museums-mcp-server (Python, MIT, 5 tools — 224,000+ artworks).
